title: MCP: Fix Cline SSE support (data loss bug)
description:
## Problem
The MCP converter incorrectly blocks SSE/HTTP servers when rendering to Cline. `renderClineMCP()` in mcp.go:564-568 skips any server with a URL, saying "Cline only supports stdio, not HTTP." Research confirms Cline supports SSE transport with `url` and `headers` fields.

Additionally, `clineMCPServerConfig` struct is missing `url` and `headers` fields, so importing a Cline config with SSE servers silently loses them.

## Root Cause
The converter was written when Cline only supported stdio. Cline has since added SSE support.

## Required Changes
1. Add `url` and `headers` fields to `clineMCPServerConfig` struct
2. Update `canonicalizeClineMCP()` to map url/headers to canonical fields
3. Remove the URL-based server skip logic in `renderClineMCP()`
4. Emit url and headers in rendered Cline config for SSE servers
5. Set type appropriately (SSE vs stdio based on presence of url)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `clineMCPServerConfig` has `URL string` and `Headers map[string]string` fields
- [ ] Canonicalizing a Cline config with SSE servers preserves url+headers in canonical output
- [ ] Rendering to Cline with SSE servers emits url+headers (no skip)
- [ ] Rendering to Cline with stdio servers still works correctly
- [ ] Existing MCP tests pass
- [ ] New test: round-trip Cline SSE config through canonicalize+render
- [ ] `make build` succeeds

## Files to Modify
- `cli/internal/converter/mcp.go` (clineMCPServerConfig, canonicalizeClineMCP, renderClineMCP)
- `cli/internal/converter/mcp_test.go` (new test cases)

## Research Reference
docs/research/mcp-research.md — Converter Coverage Audit, Issue #1 and #2
close_reason: Fixed. Cline SSE support added to canonicalize+render. All tests pass.

title: Rules: Fix Cursor globs rendering (YAML array vs comma-separated)
description:
## Problem
The Cursor rules renderer (`renderCursorRule`) outputs canonical frontmatter directly to .mdc format. The `globs` field is rendered as a YAML array (canonical format), but Cursor's native .mdc format expects a comma-separated string for the `globs` field.

Example — current output (wrong):
```yaml
globs:
- "*.ts"
- "*.tsx"
```

Expected Cursor native format:
```yaml
globs: "*.ts, *.tsx"
```

## Root Cause
renderCursorRule calls renderFrontmatter(meta) which marshals the canonical RuleMeta directly. RuleMeta.Globs is []string which YAML marshals as a sequence. Cursor expects a scalar string.

## Required Changes
1. In renderCursorRule(): convert meta.Globs []string to comma-separated string before rendering
2. Verify canonicalizeCursorRule() correctly splits comma-separated globs back to []string (it uses parse.ParseMDCFrontmatter which should already handle this)
3. Add round-trip test: Cursor globs → canonical → Cursor should preserve format

## Success Criteria
- [ ] renderCursorRule outputs globs as comma-separated string, not YAML array
- [ ] Round-trip test: Cursor rule with globs → canonicalize → render back to Cursor produces same format
- [ ] Existing rules tests pass
- [ ] New test case for multi-glob Cursor rule round-trip
- [ ] `make build` succeeds

## Files to Modify
- `cli/internal/converter/rules.go` (renderCursorRule)
- `cli/internal/converter/rules_test.go`

## Research Reference
docs/research/rules-research.md — Converter Coverage Audit, Recommended Action #7
close_reason: Fixed. Cursor globs now render as comma-separated string. MDC parser handles both formats. All tests pass.

title: MCP: Fix transport type mapping (http vs streamable-http)
description:
## Problem
Claude Code and Copilot CLI use "http" for streamable HTTP transport. The canonical format and most renderers use "streamable-http". This causes broken interop:
- Importing from Claude Code: "http" is preserved as-is (no mapping to canonical "streamable-http")
- Rendering to Claude Code: "streamable-http" is emitted, which may not be recognized

## Root Cause
The canonical format chose "streamable-http" as the internal representation but Claude Code (the primary provider) uses "http". No mapping exists between the two.

## Required Changes
1. In the default canonicalizer: map "http" → "streamable-http" on import (alongside existing httpUrl/serverUrl normalization)
2. In renderClaudeMCP(): map "streamable-http" → "http" on export
3. In renderCopilotMCP(): same mapping
4. In renderCursorMCP(): same mapping (Cursor follows Claude Code conventions)
5. Verify Gemini/Windsurf/others don't use "http" or need similar mapping

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Canonicalizing a Claude Code config with type:"http" produces canonical type:"streamable-http"
- [ ] Rendering to Claude Code with canonical type:"streamable-http" produces type:"http"
- [ ] Same for Copilot CLI and Cursor renders
- [ ] Existing MCP tests pass (update expected values where needed)
- [ ] New test: round-trip Claude Code config with HTTP transport type
- [ ] `make build` succeeds

## Files to Modify
- `cli/internal/converter/mcp.go` (Canonicalize default path, renderClaudeMCP, renderCopilotMCP, renderCursorMCP)
- `cli/internal/converter/mcp_test.go`

## Research Reference
docs/research/mcp-research.md — Converter Coverage Audit, Issue #3
close_reason: Fixed. Transport type mapping http↔streamable-http added to canonicalize+render. All tests pass.

title: Fix --json flag: output.JSON never set from CLI flag
description: main.go:40 registers --json into local jsonOutput variable, but codebase reads output.JSON (package-level bool). No PersistentPreRun bridges them. Every --json flag is silently ignored. Fix: use rootCmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVar(&output.JSON, ...) directly.
close_reason: Fixed by subagents in parallel execution

title: D1: Add required/value_type/examples to ProviderExtension struct (CLI schema)
description:
Upstream CLI schema change to unlock the docs-side provider pages redesign (syllago-docs PR, 13 tasks under phase:mvp in bd).

## What to change

Add three new fields to the ProviderExtension struct in the syllago CLI:

1. `required` — `*bool` (nullable: true = required, false = optional, nil = unspecified)
2. `value_type` — `string` (e.g., "string", "bool", "string | string[]")
3. `examples` — `[]Example` where Example is `{ code, lang, description }`

## Where

- `syllago/docs/provider-formats/<slug>.yaml` — 13 provider format files (per-provider schema definitions)
- `cli/cmd/syllago/gencapabilities.go` — aggregator that produces capabilities.json (add the new fields to the mapping)

## Why it matters

Docs-side (syllago-docs, commit 730cc75) already loosened the Zod schemas to accept these fields as optional (Task 1, D9/D10/D12). Until D1 ships, the new components render 'Unspecified' for every extension row because capabilities.json never populates them. Design doc D3 (parallel rollout) planned for this — docs can ship independently — but the user-visible payoff is gated on D1.

## Follow-ups

- D13 (bd bead: separate) — allow-list validation for these new fields in validate.go
- D14 (bd bead: separate) — GitHub issue auto-creation from validate.go warnings
- D16 (bd bead: separate) — independent, data_quality block

## References

- Design doc: docs/plans/2026-04-14-provider-pages-redesign-design.md (D1, D3, D9, D10, D12)
- Plan Out-of-Scope: docs/plans/2026-04-14-provider-pages-redesign-implementation.md:2354

description:

**Repo:** syllago-docs
**File:** scripts/sync-capabilities.ts (modify)

**What to do:**
Add three things to sync-capabilities.ts:

1. buildIssueUrl() helper (after config constants):
   Encodes a pre-filled GitHub new-issue URL with title "[capmon] Incorrect capability data: {contentType}/{keyName}" and body including key, contentType, generated_at date, and placeholder for description.

2. generateCanonicalKeyPage() function:
   Computes MetaBox props from the manifest:
   - totalProviders = count of all provider slugs
   - providerSupportCount = count of providers that support this key in this contentType
   - sourceCount = count of unique source URIs across all providers for this contentType
   - lastChangedAt = most recent last_changed_at across all providers for this contentType (fallback: manifest.generated_at)
   - issueUrl = buildIssueUrl(keyName, contentType, manifest.generated_at)
   
   Generates MDX content with:
   - Frontmatter: title (keyName), description (first sentence of meta.description)
   - AUTO-GENERATED comment
   - Imports for MetaBox and CanonicalSupportTable (relative path: ../../../../components/...)
   - <MetaBox .../> component call with all props
   - meta.description paragraph
   - **Type:** `{meta.type}` **Content type:** `{contentType}` line
   - ## Provider Support heading
   - <CanonicalSupportTable keyName="{keyName}" contentType="{contentType}" /> call

3. generateCanonicalKeyPages() function:
   Clears + recreates CANONICAL_KEYS_MDX_DIR, iterates manifest.canonical_keys, converts keyName underscores to hyphens for slug, writes {slug}.mdx.

4. Update main() — replace the placeholder console.log line with:

title: ACP Spec: Final validation pass
description:
## What

Final validation of the complete spec after all implementation and per-bead validation is done. This is a comprehensive audit — not just checking individual changes, but verifying the spec works as a coherent whole.

## Validation Checklist

### 1. Internal consistency

- All section cross-references are valid (no broken "see Section X" pointing to renumbered/removed sections)
- All normative references in Section 12.1 are actually cited in the spec
- All informative references in Section 12.2 are actually cited
- No dangling references to sections or appendices that don't exist
- Section numbering is sequential with no gaps or duplicates

### 2. Test vector integrity

- TV-MH and TV-MH2 hashes are still correct after Section 8 rewrite (allowlist matches TV-MH fields)
- TV-SI signing input hash is correct for the new ACP-V1: format
- All other test vectors (TV-01 through TV-16) are unchanged
- Test vectors use consistent formatting (hex format, hash format, section references)

### 3. Normative language audit

- Every MUST/MUST NOT/SHALL/SHOULD/MAY is used correctly per BCP 14
- No MUST in informative sections (appendices, notes, Security Considerations)
- No "should" (lowercase) used where "SHOULD" (uppercase) is intended
- Requirements Language section (Section 4) is unchanged

### 4. Decision implementation audit

Verify each of the 13 decisions (T1-T13) is correctly reflected:

| Decision | Expected | Section |
|----------|----------|---------|
| T1: Domain separator | ACP-V1: prefix in signing input | 9.1 |
| T2: Explicit allowlist | meta_version 1 field list in Section 8 | 8 |
| T3: Reject undefined | Non-normative note, implementation decides UX | 5.2.1 |
| T4: Conformance classes | Strict + Permissive subsections | 5.2.2, 5.2.3 |
| T5: Integer versioning | Unchanged + rationale note | 6.3.4 |
| T6+T7: VCS exclusions | .git/.svn/.hg/CVS excluded, no .acpignore | 7.5 |
| T8: Offline Sigstore | SSH recommended for air-gapped | 9.3 or 11 |
| T9: Identity verification | MUST verify, algorithm implementation-defined | 9.2 |
| T10: Authors no normalization | Documented, SHOULD be consistent | 6.3.6 |
| T11: No deprecated/yanked | Deferred to registries | 11 |
| T12: Name constraints | 1-128 chars, allowed char set | 6.3.3 |
| T13: CRLF | Raw bytes + implementation note + rationale | 7.5 |

### 5. Mechanical fixes audit

Verify all 8 mechanical fixes (M1-M8) are applied.

### 6. Interoperability requirements audit

Verify all 6 interoperability items (I1-I6) are addressed.

### 7. Spec readability

- New sections flow logically from existing structure
- Section 11 doesn't feel bloated or repetitive (subsections are well-scoped)
- YAML-to-JSON type mapping table is readable and complete
- Conformance classes are clear about what each level requires

### 8. Markdown formatting

- All tables render correctly
- All code blocks are properly fenced
- All headings are sequential (no ### after #)
- No trailing whitespace issues
- File passes markdownlint basic rules

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All 8 audit categories pass
- [ ] No changes needed — this is a read-only validation
- [ ] If issues are found, report them with exact locations and fixes needed
- [ ] Final verdict: PASS (ready for commit) or FAIL (with specific blockers)
close_reason: PASS — all 6 audit categories pass. 13/13 decisions implemented, 8/8 mechanical fixes confirmed, all test vectors mathematically verified, section numbering sequential, cross-references valid, markdown structure clean. Two minor non-blocking style notes documented.

title: Add tag protection rule for v* release tags
description:
## Why
No tag protection exists. Anyone with write access can create or overwrite tags. Since release.yml triggers on v* tags and has contents:write + id-token:write permissions, unauthorized tag creation could trigger a malicious release build.

## What
Restrict v* tag creation to repo admins/maintainers only.

## How
- Option A (rulesets): Settings > Rules > Rulesets > New ruleset > target tags matching "v*" > restrict creation to admins
- Option B (tag protection): Settings > Tags > Add rule > "v*" pattern
- Option A is preferred (rulesets are more flexible and support bypass lists)

## Success Criteria
- Only authorized users can create v* tags
- Unauthorized tag push attempts are rejected
- Release workflow cannot be triggered by non-maintainers

## Blocked By
Repo must be public for full ruleset features
close_reason: Configured via GitHub API — status checks require golangci-lint + Go Test + Build; tag ruleset protects v* tags

description:

## How
gh api -X PUT repos/OpenScribbler/syllago/branches/main/protection with required_status_checks for golangci-lint and Go Test + Build (strict: true).

Was configured and confirmed working on 2026-03-26, then disabled for pre-release iteration speed.

title: M1: Registry sync missing git hardening
description:
## Why
registry.Sync() runs git pull --ff-only without the security hardening that Clone() applies. Missing: GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1, core.hooksPath=/dev/null, --no-recurse-submodules. A registry could add a post-merge git hook in an update — on the next sync, that hook executes with the user's permissions. This is a code execution vulnerability triggered by normal usage.

## What
Apply identical git hardening to Sync() as Clone() already uses.

## How (general)
- In registry.go Sync(), add GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1 to cmd.Env
- Add -c core.hooksPath=/dev/null before the pull subcommand
- Add --no-recurse-submodules flag
- Consider extracting a helper function that applies hardening to any git command

## Success Criteria
- Sync() applies the same env vars and config flags as Clone()
- Post-merge hooks in registries do NOT execute during sync
- Submodules are not fetched during sync

## Testing
- Unit test: create a test registry with a post-merge hook, verify it doesn't execute on sync
- Unit test: verify GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1 is set in Sync() command environment
- Regression test: normal sync still works with hardening applied

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — Section M1
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

title: H2: MCP install silently overwrites user-defined server keys
description:
## Why
installMCP uses sjson.SetRawBytes which unconditionally overwrites any existing MCP server entry with the same name. No collision check exists. A .bak file is created but the user isn't warned. If a user has manually configured an MCP server and installs content with a same-named server, their config is silently lost. On uninstall, syllago deletes the key entirely — original config gone.

## What
Add collision detection to MCP install. Check if a server key already exists and wasn't installed by syllago before overwriting.

## How (general)
- Before sjson.SetRawBytes in mcp.go:334, check gjson.GetBytes(fileData, key).Exists()
- Cross-reference with installed.json to determine if the existing key was syllago-managed
- If collision with user-managed key: warn prominently and require --force flag
- If collision with syllago-managed key: overwrite (normal update behavior)
- Add --force flag to install command for explicit override

## Success Criteria
- Installing content with an existing user-defined MCP server name shows a warning and aborts without --force
- Installing content with an existing syllago-managed server name succeeds normally
- User's original config is preserved when collision is detected

## Testing
- Unit test: install MCP server, manually add same-named server to settings, attempt reinstall — verify rejection
- Unit test: install, uninstall, reinstall same-named server — verify success (syllago-managed)
- Unit test: --force flag overrides collision check
- Integration test: verify .bak file contains original config

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — Section H2
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

title: H1: Self-update binary download without signature verification
description:
## Why
syllago update downloads a binary from GitHub Releases and replaces the running executable. SHA-256 checksum verification exists, but checksums.txt is downloaded from the same source as the binary. If GitHub Releases is compromised (account takeover, CI compromise) or MITM'd, an attacker controls both the binary and the checksums. The signing package has interfaces defined but is unimplemented.

Same-origin checksums only catch accidental corruption, not deliberate tampering. Real supply-chain security requires the verification key to come from a different trust root than the artifact.

## What
Implement cryptographic signature verification for self-update binaries. The signing package at cli/internal/signing/signing.go already has interfaces defined — implement them.

## How (general)
- Implement the signing package (cosign, GPG, or minisign)
- Sign checksums.txt during CI release builds with a key NOT stored alongside the release
- Hardcode the public verification key in the binary itself
- Verify signature before applying update
- Reject updates that fail signature verification
- Alternative for initial release: disable auto-update and document manual verification

## Success Criteria
- Binary updates are verified against a signature from a different trust root than GitHub Releases
- Tampered binaries or checksums are rejected with a clear error message
- Update flow works end-to-end with signature verification

## Testing
- Unit tests: verify signature check passes with valid signature, fails with tampered binary, fails with tampered checksum
- Integration test: mock GitHub API returning modified binary, verify rejection
- Test with expired/revoked key handling

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — Section H1
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

title: Per-server MCP config preview support
description:
## Context

Depends on: syllago-8ihnl (explode MCP configs), syllago-j3q8c (individual install)

After syllago-j3q8c adds ParseMCPServerConfig to the installer, this bead is effectively ABSORBED into syllago-j3q8c. The ParseMCPServerConfig function IS the preview support — it extracts the MCPConfig for a specific server key so the TUI can display command/url/env for that server.

## Remaining work (if any)

Verify that after syllago-j3q8c is complete:
1. ParseMCPServerConfig(itemPath, serverKey) returns correct MCPConfig for any server
2. The existing TUI detail view (detail_render.go line 235) can call it with item.ServerKey instead of the old ParseMCPConfig
3. CheckEnvVars works correctly with the per-server MCPConfig

If syllago-j3q8c fully implements ParseMCPServerConfig with tests, this bead can be closed immediately after verification.

## Files involved

- \`cli/internal/installer/mcp.go\` — ParseMCPServerConfig (added by syllago-j3q8c)
- \`cli/internal/tui/detail.go\` — where mcpConfig is loaded for the detail view (will need to call ParseMCPServerConfig instead of ParseMCPConfig)
- \`cli/internal/tui/detail_render.go\` — renders MCP server config preview (line 234-265)

## Note on TUI changes

The TUI is being completely redesigned in a parallel session. The detail_render.go change (swapping ParseMCPConfig → ParseMCPServerConfig) may be handled by the redesign. If not, it's a one-line change. Either way, the logic (ParseMCPServerConfig) must exist for the redesigned TUI to use.

## Success criteria

- ParseMCPServerConfig exists and is tested (may be done by syllago-j3q8c)
- TUI can display correct config for individual MCP server items
close_reason: Absorbed into syllago-j3q8c. ParseMCPServerConfig was implemented there and verified.

title: Install/uninstall individual MCP servers
description:
## Context

Depends on: syllago-8ihnl (explode MCP configs into per-server items)

Currently installMCP/uninstallMCP in installer/mcp.go operate on ALL server entries in a config.json at once. After syllago-8ihnl, each ContentItem represents a single server (via item.ServerKey), so install/uninstall must operate on just that one server.

## Provider-Specific MCP Architecture (DO NOT CHANGE)

The existing provider infrastructure handles all the complexity of where and how MCP configs are stored. This code stays untouched — the per-server change only narrows WHICH entries get merged/removed within the existing flow.

### Config file locations (mcpConfigPath, line 54-82)

| Provider | Config File Path | Scope |
|----------|-----------------|-------|
| claude-code | ~/.claude.json | Global |
| claude-code | .mcp.json (project root) | Project (also checked by FindMCPLocations) |
| gemini-cli | ~/.config/gemini-cli/settings.json | Global |
| copilot-cli | .copilot/mcp.json (project root) | Project |
| kiro | .kiro/settings/mcp.json (project root) | Project |
| opencode | opencode.json (project root) | Project |
| zed | ~/.config/zed/settings.json | Global |
| cline | VS Code extension path (dynamic) | Global |
| roo-code | .roo/mcp.json (project root) | Project |

### JSON keys (MCPConfigKey + FindMCPLocations overrides)

| Provider | JSON key for server entries |
|----------|---------------------------|
| Most providers | "mcpServers" |
| Zed | "context_servers" |
| OpenCode | "mcp" |
| Amp | "amp.mcpServers" |

### Special handling

- **OpenCode**: JSONC comment stripping via converter.StripJSONCComments before parsing (readMCPConfig, line 200-208)
- **Claude Code**: FindMCPLocations checks THREE locations: settings.json files (global+project), ~/.claude.json, and .mcp.json
- **Provider config files may contain OTHER data** (e.g., Zed's settings.json has editor settings alongside context_servers; Claude Code's .claude.json has permissions alongside mcpServers). Install/uninstall must only touch the server key, not other content.

### Existing functions that stay unchanged

| Function | Purpose | Why unchanged |
|----------|---------|--------------|
| mcpConfigPath() | Returns target config file path per provider | Path resolution is independent of server count |
| MCPConfigKey() | Returns JSON key per provider | Key is the same whether installing 1 or N servers |
| readMCPConfig() | Reads + optionally strips JSONC | Reading is the same |
| ExtractServerEntries() | Parses config.json into server map | Still needed to get the specific server's config data |
| FindMCPLocations() | Finds all files where MCP configs exist | Used by TUI for status display, unaffected |
| backupFile() | Backs up before modification | Same flow |
| writeJSONFile() | Writes result | Same flow |

## What Changes

### 1. installMCP (line 260-324)

**Current**: Calls ExtractServerEntries → iterates ALL entries → merges ALL into provider config → records one InstalledMCP with all ServerNames.

**New**: Calls ExtractServerEntries → filters to ONLY item.ServerKey → merges that one entry → records one InstalledMCP with that single server name.

```go
// Current (installs all):
entries, err := ExtractServerEntries(rawData, item.Name, jsonKey)
for name, configData := range entries { ... }

// New (installs one):
entries, err := ExtractServerEntries(rawData, item.Name, jsonKey)
serverKey := item.ServerKey
if serverKey == "" {
    serverKey = item.Name // backward compat for flat format
}
configData, ok := entries[serverKey]
if !ok {
    return "", fmt.Errorf("server %q not found in config.json", serverKey)
}
// merge only configData for serverKey
```

**Backward compat**: If item.ServerKey is empty (flat format items, or items from before this change), fall back to installing all entries (current behavior).

### 2. uninstallMCP (line 326-381)

**Current**: Finds InstalledMCP by item.Name → removes ALL tracked ServerNames from provider config → removes the InstalledMCP entry.

**New**: Finds InstalledMCP by item.Name AND ServerKey → removes only that server key → removes that specific InstalledMCP entry.

### 3. checkMCPStatus (line 383-420)

**Current**: Checks if ANY tracked server name exists in provider config.

**New**: When item.ServerKey is set, check if THAT specific server key exists. Fallback to current behavior when ServerKey is empty.

### 4. InstalledMCP struct (installed.go, line 26-31)

Add ServerKey field:
```go
type InstalledMCP struct {
    Name        string    `json:"name"`                  // content item directory name (provenance)
    ServerKey   string    `json:"serverKey,omitempty"`    // specific server key installed
    ServerNames []string  `json:"serverNames,omitempty"`  // DEPRECATED: kept for backward compat
    Source      string    `json:"source"`
    InstalledAt time.Time `json:"installedAt"`
}
```

New installs populate ServerKey (single string). Old installs still have ServerNames (slice). Uninstall must handle both formats.

### 5. FindMCP (installed.go, line 96-104)

**Current**: Matches by Name only.

**New**: Add FindMCPByServerKey(name, serverKey) that matches both. Keep FindMCP for backward compat (used by uninstall to find legacy entries).

### 6. ParseMCPServerConfig — new function

```go
func ParseMCPServerConfig(itemPath string, serverKey string) (*MCPConfig, error)
```

Reads config.json, extracts the specific server entry from nested format, returns its MCPConfig. Falls back to flat parsing when serverKey is empty. This is used by the TUI detail view to show the correct server's command/url/env.

## Files to modify

- `cli/internal/installer/mcp.go` — installMCP, uninstallMCP, checkMCPStatus, add ParseMCPServerConfig
- `cli/internal/installer/installed.go` — InstalledMCP struct (add ServerKey), add FindMCPByServerKey
- `cli/internal/installer/mcp_test.go` — new tests + update existing
- `cli/internal/installer/installed_test.go` — test FindMCPByServerKey

## Testing

- Unit test: install single server from nested config (2+ servers) → only that server appears in provider config, others absent
- Unit test: install two servers independently from same config → both tracked as separate InstalledMCP entries
- Unit test: uninstall one server → other server remains in provider config and in installed.json
- Unit test: checkMCPStatus returns StatusInstalled for installed server, StatusNotInstalled for non-installed server from same config
- Unit test: ParseMCPServerConfig extracts correct MCPConfig for given key (command, args, env, url fields)
- Unit test: backward compat — empty ServerKey installs all entries (current behavior preserved)
- Unit test: backward compat — uninstall works for old InstalledMCP entries with ServerNames but no ServerKey
- Unit test: provider-specific JSON keys work correctly (test with "mcpServers" and "context_servers" at minimum)
- Integration test: install server A from config X, install server B from config X, uninstall A → B still present in provider config
- Existing MCP install/uninstall/status tests still pass

## Success criteria

- Individual servers can be installed and uninstalled independently
- installed.json tracks each server with its own InstalledMCP entry (using ServerKey field)
- No regression for flat-format MCP configs (single server, no wrapper)
- Backward compatible with existing installed.json entries (old ServerNames format still uninstalls correctly)
- Provider-specific config paths, JSON keys, and special handling (JSONC stripping, multi-location) are completely untouched
- ParseMCPServerConfig can extract config for any individual server for TUI preview
close_reason: Implemented and verified. installMCP/uninstallMCP/checkMCPStatus support per-server operations via item.ServerKey. ParseMCPServerConfig added. InstalledMCP has ServerKey field. FindMCPByServerKey added. All backward compat preserved. 5 new test functions.

title: Explode nested MCP configs into per-server catalog items
description:
## Context

MCP config items are currently discovered as one ContentItem per directory. A single config.json can contain multiple MCP servers in nested format:

\`\`\`json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "server-a": { "command": "npx", "args": [...] },
    "server-b": { "url": "https://..." }
  }
}
\`\`\`

This produces ONE item "example-kitchen-sink-mcp". Users cannot see or install individual servers.

## Goal

At catalog scan time, explode nested MCP configs so each server entry becomes its own ContentItem. This enables individual server visibility, selection, and installation.

## Current Architecture

### Scanner flow (catalog/scanner.go)

MCP is a "universal" content type, scanned by \`scanUniversal()\` (line 350-421). The function:
1. Iterates subdirectories under \`mcp/\`
2. Validates directory names (\`IsValidItemName\`)
3. Creates one ContentItem per directory
4. For Skills/Agents: extracts name/description from frontmatter
5. **For MCP (falls into \`default\` case, line 404): does nothing extra** — no description, no server parsing
6. Collects file listing, loads metadata, appends to catalog

### ContentItem struct (catalog/types.go, line 62-75)

\`\`\`go
type ContentItem struct {
    Name        string
    DisplayName string
    Description string
    Type        ContentType
    Path        string         // absolute path to item directory
    Provider    string         // for provider-specific content
    Files       []string
    Meta        *metadata.Meta
    Library     bool
    Registry    string
    Source      string
}
\`\`\`

No \`ServerKey\` field exists yet.

### Import dependencies

- \`catalog\` imports: metadata, gjson, yaml (and stdlib)
- \`catalog\` does NOT import \`installer\`
- \`installer\` imports \`catalog\`
- **Cannot call installer.ExtractServerEntries from catalog** — would create circular import

### config.json formats (two variants)

**Nested format** (multiple servers):
\`\`\`json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "filesystem": { "command": "npx", "args": [...] },
    "http-server": { "url": "https://..." }
  }
}
\`\`\`

**Flat format** (single server):
\`\`\`json
{
  "command": "npx",
  "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem"]
}
\`\`\`

### JSON key variations by provider

The nested wrapper key varies by provider (see installer/mcp.go MCPConfigKey):
- Most providers: \`"mcpServers"\`
- Zed: \`"context_servers"\`
- OpenCode: \`"mcp"\`
- Amp: \`"amp.mcpServers"\`

**Important for scanner**: The scanner reads the CONTENT REPO's config.json, which uses the canonical format (\`"mcpServers"\` wrapper). Provider-specific keys are only relevant at install time. The scanner should check for \`"mcpServers"\` as the canonical wrapper key.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Add ServerKey field to ContentItem (catalog/types.go)

\`\`\`go
type ContentItem struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    ServerKey   string  // For MCP: which server entry in config.json this item represents
}
\`\`\`

### 2. Modify scanUniversal (catalog/scanner.go, line 377-406)

Replace the \`default\` case with MCP-specific logic:

\`\`\`go
case MCP:
    // Read config.json and check for nested format
    configPath := filepath.Join(itemDir, "config.json")
    data, err := os.ReadFile(configPath)
    if err != nil {
        // No config.json — emit single item with directory name (graceful fallback)
        break
    }

    // Check for nested format: {"mcpServers": {"name": {...}, ...}}
    servers := gjson.GetBytes(data, "mcpServers")
    if servers.Exists() && servers.IsObject() {
        // Explode: emit one ContentItem per server entry
        servers.ForEach(func(key, value gjson.Result) bool {
            serverName := key.String()
            if !IsValidItemName(serverName) {
                cat.Warnings = append(cat.Warnings, fmt.Sprintf(
                    "skipping MCP server %q in %s — name unsafe for JSON keys", serverName, entry.Name()))
                return true
            }
            serverItem := ContentItem{
                Name:        serverName,
                Type:        MCP,
                Path:        itemDir,  // still points to parent dir (needed for install)
                ServerKey:   serverName,
                Library:     local,
                Description: mcpServerDescription(value),
                Files:       item.Files,
                Meta:        item.Meta,
            }
            cat.Items = append(cat.Items, serverItem)
            return true
        })
        // Skip appending the parent item — individual servers replace it
        continue  // skip the cat.Items = append at end of loop
    }

    // Flat format — single server, use directory name
    item.ServerKey = entry.Name()
    item.Description = mcpFlatDescription(data)
\`\`\`

**Loop control note**: The \`continue\` after exploding nested servers skips the \`cat.Items = append(cat.Items, item)\` at line 418 (which would add the parent directory as an item). For flat format, the item falls through to the normal append.

### 3. Add description helpers (catalog/scanner.go)

\`\`\`go
// mcpServerDescription generates a short description from a server config value.
func mcpServerDescription(value gjson.Result) string {
    if cmd := value.Get("command").String(); cmd != "" {
        args := value.Get("args").String()
        if args != "" {
            // Show first meaningful arg (skip flags)
            // e.g., "npx @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem"
        }
        return cmd  // truncate to ~60 chars
    }
    if url := value.Get("url").String(); url != "" {
        return url  // truncate to ~60 chars
    }
    return ""
}

// mcpFlatDescription generates a description from a flat config.json.
func mcpFlatDescription(data []byte) string {
    if cmd := gjson.GetBytes(data, "command").String(); cmd != "" {
        return cmd
    }
    if url := gjson.GetBytes(data, "url").String(); url != "" {
        return url
    }
    return ""
}
\`\`\`

### 4. Handle metadata and files for exploded items

When exploding nested configs, each server item shares:
- \`Path\`: same parent directory (needed to find config.json at install time)
- \`Files\`: same file listing (config.json, README, etc.)
- \`Meta\`: same .syllago.yaml metadata
- \`Library\`: same as parent
- \`Registry\`: set later by the caller (same as parent)

### 5. Naming collision handling

If two MCP config directories contain servers with the same name (e.g., both have a "filesystem" server), they'll appear as two items with the same Name but different Paths. The catalog already handles this via its override/shadowing logic (project > library > registry precedence). No special handling needed.

## Files to modify

- \`cli/internal/catalog/types.go\` — add \`ServerKey\` field to ContentItem
- \`cli/internal/catalog/scanner.go\` — MCP case in scanUniversal, add description helpers
- \`cli/internal/catalog/scanner_test.go\` — new tests for MCP explosion

## Testing

- Unit test: nested config.json with 2 servers → produces 2 ContentItems with correct Names=server keys, ServerKeys=server keys, Paths=parent dir
- Unit test: nested config.json with 1 server → produces 1 ContentItem with server key as Name
- Unit test: flat config.json → produces 1 ContentItem with Name=directory name, ServerKey=directory name
- Unit test: no config.json → produces 1 ContentItem with Name=directory name, ServerKey empty (graceful fallback)
- Unit test: nested config with invalid server name → warning emitted, server skipped, other servers still emitted
- Unit test: description shows command for stdio servers, URL for HTTP servers
- Unit test: description is empty string for configs with neither command nor URL
- Unit test: Files and Meta are shared across exploded items
- Verify \`syllago list mcp\` shows individual server names (integration-level confirmation)
- Existing catalog tests still pass (Skills, Agents, Rules, Hooks, Commands unaffected)

## Success criteria

- \`catalog.ByType(MCP)\` returns individual server items, not directory-level items, for nested configs
- Each server item has ServerKey populated
- Flat-format configs still work (one item per directory)
- No circular imports between catalog and installer
- Descriptions are auto-generated from command/URL
- No regression in non-MCP content type scanning
close_reason: Implemented and verified. ServerKey field added to ContentItem, scanUniversal explodes nested MCP configs into per-server items, description helpers generate from command/URL. 7 test functions cover all cases. Coverage 80.9%.

title: Add SourceScope and SourceProject to metadata
description:
## Problem
The metadata system has no way to track whether content was imported from a global or project scope, or which project it came from. This is needed for both the hooks scope fix and the MCP add pathway to handle name collisions gracefully.

## Implementation
Add two new fields to the `Meta` struct in `cli/internal/metadata/metadata.go`:

```go
SourceScope   string `yaml:"source_scope,omitempty"`   // "global" or "project"
SourceProject string `yaml:"source_project,omitempty"` // project directory name (only when scope is "project")
```

- `SourceScope`: either `"global"` or `"project"`
- `SourceProject`: derived from `filepath.Base(projectRoot)` when scope is project (e.g., `"demo-project"`)

## Display Format
- Global items: `(global)`
- Project items: `(project/demo-project)`
- Used in CLI discovery output and TUI parenthetical badges

## Files to Modify
- `cli/internal/metadata/metadata.go` — add fields to Meta struct

## Testing
- Unit test: metadata round-trip (save + load) with scope fields populated
- Unit test: scope fields omitted from YAML when empty (omitempty)

## Success Criteria
- Meta struct has SourceScope and SourceProject fields
- Fields serialize/deserialize correctly in YAML
- Empty fields don't appear in output (omitempty)
- Existing metadata tests still pass
close_reason: All 4 success criteria validated by agent: fields exist, YAML round-trip works, omitempty correct, all 27 tests pass

title: Implement MCP config discovery and add pathway
description:
## Problem
`syllago add mcp --from claude-code` produces zero output and adds nothing. The MCP content type has no add/discovery implementation — it was never built. The code skips MCP in both discovery and add paths.

## Root Cause Analysis
1. **Discovery (`runAddDiscovery`, add_cmd.go:384):** Only calls `discoverHooksForDisplay()` to merge settings.json content. No equivalent `discoverMcpForDisplay()` exists. MCP configs are invisible in discovery.
2. **Add (`runAdd`, add_cmd.go:154):** Special-case branch only handles `catalog.Hooks`. No `catalog.MCP` branch. Falls through to file-based `DiscoverFromProvider`.
3. **`DiscoverFromProvider` (add.go:479):** Explicitly skips MCP: `if ct == catalog.Hooks || ct == catalog.MCP { continue }`.
4. **`--all` path (add_cmd.go:154):** Enters the hooks block for `--all` but inner condition only matches hooks, so MCP is never handled.

## MCP Config Location Matrix (per provider)
MCP configs live in DIFFERENT files per provider. Discovery must check all sources:

| Provider | MCP Files | JSON Key |
|----------|-----------|----------|
| claude-code | settings.json (global+project), ~/.claude.json, .mcp.json | mcpServers |
| gemini-cli | settings.json | mcpServers |
| copilot-cli | .copilot/mcp.json | mcpServers |
| kiro | .kiro/settings/mcp.json | mcpServers |
| opencode | opencode.json | mcp |
| zed | settings.json | context_servers |
| cline | VS Code settings path | mcpServers |
| roo-code | .roo/mcp.json | mcpServers |
| cursor | .cursor/mcp.json | mcpServers |
| windsurf | mcp_config.json | mcpServers |
| amp | settings.json | amp.mcpServers |

## Implementation Plan

### 1. New `FindMCPLocations` function (installer/scope.go or installer/mcp.go)
Returns all files where MCP configs exist for a provider, with scope labels:
- For settings.json-based providers: use `FindSettingsLocationsWithBase` + extract mcpServers key
- For dedicated-file providers: use `mcpConfigPath` 
- For claude-code: BOTH settings.json files AND dedicated files (~/.claude.json, .mcp.json)
- Returns []MCPLocation with Path, Scope (global/project), ProjectName fields

### 2. New `discoverMcpForDisplay` function (add_cmd.go)
Mirrors `discoverHooksForDisplay`:
- Calls `FindMCPLocations` for the provider
- Reads each file, extracts individual server entries using `installer.MCPConfigKey` + gjson
- Returns []add.DiscoveryItem with scope metadata
- No dedup — shows all scopes

### 3. New `runAddMcp` function (add_cmd.go)
Mirrors `runAddHooks`:
- Finds MCP locations, reads configs, extracts servers
- Writes each server to library: `~/.syllago/content/mcp/<provider>/<server-name>/config.json`
- config.json format: `{"mcpServers": {"name": {...}}}` wrapper (matches installer expectations)
- Writes metadata with SourceScope and SourceProject
- Collision handling: suffix with -2, -3 on name conflicts
- Respects --scope, --exclude, --force, --dry-run flags

### 4. Wire into existing code paths
- `runAddDiscovery`: add `discoverMcpForDisplay` call alongside `discoverHooksForDisplay`
- `runAdd`: add `typeStr == string(catalog.MCP)` branch alongside hooks
- `--all` path: ensure MCP is included when adding all content

## Existing Infrastructure to Reuse
- `installer.MCPConfigKey(prov)` — returns the JSON key per provider
- `installer.ExtractServerEntries(data, name, key)` — parses both nested and flat MCP formats
- `installer.mcpConfigPath(prov, repoRoot)` — returns dedicated MCP file path per provider  
- `installer.FindSettingsLocationsWithBase(prov, root, baseDir)` — finds settings.json files
- `converter.StripJSONCComments(data)` — needed for OpenCode JSONC format

## Files to Modify
- `cli/cmd/syllago/add_cmd.go` — new functions + wiring
- `cli/internal/installer/mcp.go` or `cli/internal/installer/scope.go` — FindMCPLocations
- `cli/internal/metadata/metadata.go` — SourceScope + SourceProject (shared with hooks bead)

## Testing
- Unit test: `discoverMcpForDisplay` with claude-code provider having MCP in settings.json — verify servers found
- Unit test: `discoverMcpForDisplay` with dedicated-file provider (e.g., roo-code) — verify servers found
- Unit test: `runAddMcp` writes correct library structure (config.json + metadata)
- Unit test: name collision between global and project MCP servers — verify suffix + metadata
- Unit test: `--all` flag includes MCP servers alongside other content types
- Unit test: `--exclude` flag filters out named MCP servers
- Unit test: discovery mode shows MCP servers with scope labels
- Manual test: `syllago add mcp --from claude-code` in ~/demo-project — should find obsidian from project settings.json
- Manual test: `syllago add --from claude-code` — should show MCP servers in discovery output

## Success Criteria
- `syllago add --from claude-code` (discovery) shows MCP servers from all locations with scope labels
- `syllago add mcp --from claude-code` adds MCP servers to library with correct config.json format
- `syllago add --all --from claude-code` includes MCP servers
- MCP servers from different scopes are individually identifiable via metadata
- Name collisions handled gracefully with suffix
- All existing tests pass
- New tests cover MCP discovery, add, collision, and multi-provider scenarios
- Works for all providers in the matrix (at least claude-code, gemini-cli, and one dedicated-file provider tested)
close_reason: All 8 criteria now pass including test coverage. 5 new tests: TestAddMcpDiscovery, TestAddMcpWritesToGlobalDir, TestAddMcpScopeMetadata, TestAddMcpCollision, TestAddMcpDryRun. Plus 4 FindMCPLocations tests in installer. Full suite passes.

title: Fix hooks --scope default to 'all'
description:
## Problem
`syllago add hooks --from claude-code` only reads global settings.json, ignoring project-level `.claude/settings.json`. Discovery correctly finds project-level hooks but the add pathway skips them.

## Root Cause
In `cli/cmd/syllago/add_cmd.go:60`, the `--scope` flag defaults to `"global"`. The `runAddHooks` function (lines 606-611) filters settings locations by scope, so project-level settings are filtered out. Meanwhile, `discoverHooksForDisplay` iterates ALL locations without filtering, so discovery shows project hooks correctly.

## Fix
1. Change `--scope` default from `"global"` to `"all"` in `add_cmd.go:60`
2. Update `discoverHooksForDisplay` to stop deduplicating by name (currently uses `seen[name]` which drops the second scope's version)
3. Add `SourceScope` and `SourceProject` fields to metadata.Meta struct
4. When adding hooks, populate scope metadata (global vs project, with project name derived from projectRoot dirname)
5. On name collision in library (same hook name from different scopes): suffix with `-2`, `-3`, etc.
6. CLI output: show scope indicator e.g. `(global)` or `(project/demo-project)`

## Files to Modify
- `cli/cmd/syllago/add_cmd.go` — scope default, discoverHooksForDisplay dedup logic, scope metadata in addHooksFromLocation
- `cli/internal/metadata/metadata.go` — add SourceScope and SourceProject fields

## Testing
- Unit test: `runAddHooks` with both global and project settings.json containing hooks — verify both scopes are added
- Unit test: same hook name in both scopes — verify collision suffix and correct scope metadata
- Unit test: `discoverHooksForDisplay` with both scopes — verify both appear in output
- Manual test: `syllago add hooks --from claude-code` in ~/demo-project — should find PreToolUse/Bash and PostToolUse/Edit from project settings.json

## Success Criteria
- `syllago add --from claude-code` (discovery) shows hooks from BOTH global and project scopes with scope labels
- `syllago add hooks --from claude-code` adds hooks from both scopes
- Same-name hooks from different scopes get unique library paths (suffix) and correct scope metadata
- `--scope=global` still works to filter to global only
- All existing hook tests pass
- New tests cover the scope-aware behavior
close_reason: All criteria pass. Scope default changed to 'all', scope metadata written, collision handling works. 2 new tests: TestAddHooksScopeDefaultIsAll, TestAddHooksScopeMetadata. All existing tests pass.

title: Hook import ignores project-level settings.json
description:
syllago add hooks --from claude-code only reads the global ~/.claude/settings.json, ignoring project-level .claude/settings.json hooks. Discovery (syllago add --from claude-code) correctly finds project-level hooks, but the actual add pathway skips them.

This is a duplicate/escalation of syllago-ydai (previously P2), escalating to P1 because it blocks the demo and means project-specific hooks cannot be imported.

Reproduction:
1. Create project with .claude/settings.json containing hooks
2. Run: syllago add hooks --from claude-code  
3. Result: only global hooks are added, project hooks are silently ignored
4. Run: syllago add --from claude-code (discovery)
5. Result: project hooks ARE shown in discovery but cannot be added
close_reason: Superseded by syllago-1srjf which has full implementation plan

title: MCP config import is completely broken (silent failure)
description:
syllago add mcp --from claude-code produces no output and adds nothing. MCP configs don't appear in discovery output either. The mcpServers section in settings.json (both global and project-level) is not being read by the add/discovery code path.

syllago add --all also skips MCP configs entirely.

Reproduction:
1. Have a .claude/settings.json with mcpServers section (e.g. obsidian MCP)
2. Run: syllago add mcp --from claude-code
3. Result: no output, nothing added
4. Run: syllago add --from claude-code
5. Result: MCP not listed in discovery output

This is a P1 because MCP configs are a core content type and a key selling point.
close_reason: Superseded by syllago-hdb4r which has full implementation plan

title: Fix symlink escape in sandbox config staging
description:
In configdiff.go copyDir(), symlinks are preserved as-is during config staging. A malicious project config directory (e.g. .claude/) could contain symlinks pointing outside the project directory (e.g. '~/.claude/config.json -> ../../sensitive.json'). When the sandbox modifies and ApplyDiff copies back, the target outside sandbox scope gets overwritten.

Fix: In copyDir(), resolve all symlinks to their targets BEFORE copying. Verify targets are within the original source directory. Reject symlinks that escape.

Files: cli/internal/sandbox/configdiff.go (lines 336-349)
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix hook script security: absolute paths + permissions
description:
Two issues in hook script installation:

1. ABSOLUTE PATH ACCEPTANCE (hooks.go:319-321): The resolveHookScripts() function accepts absolute file paths in hook command references via filepath.IsAbs(). A malicious registry can craft hooks referencing system files (e.g. '/etc/passwd'). Fix: Only accept relative paths within the item directory. Reject absolute paths entirely.

2. PERMISSIONS TOO BROAD (hooks.go:356): Hook scripts installed with 0755 (world-executable). Fix: Change to 0700 (owner-only executable).

Files: cli/internal/installer/hooks.go
close_reason: Closed

title: Harden git wrapper subcommand parsing
description:
The sandbox git wrapper only checks $1 for blocked subcommands, making it bypassable via git flags preceding the subcommand (e.g. 'git -c user.name=x push'). Additionally, dangerous git env vars (GIT_SSH_COMMAND, GIT_EXEC_PATH, GIT_PROTOCOL_HANDLER_*) are not blocked, and git aliases could bypass the subcommand check entirely.

Fix:
1. Scan all args for subcommands, not just $1
2. Block dangerous git env vars in envfilter.go or the wrapper itself
3. Disable alias expansion (GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1 + unset GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL or set to sandbox-safe config)
4. Consider using '--' to separate flags from subcommands

Files: cli/internal/sandbox/gitwrapper.go, cli/internal/sandbox/envfilter.go
close_reason: Closed

title: 4.1: Wire StructuredError into promote/privacy.go
description:
File: cli/internal/promote/privacy.go (modify)

Convert the two privacy gate error returns from plain fmt.Errorf to output.StructuredError.

CheckPrivacyGate (G1 gate, line 44):
- Currently: fmt.Errorf("cannot publish %q to registry %q\n\n  Content origin: ...")
- Change to: output.NewStructuredErrorDetail(output.ErrPrivacyPublishBlocked, "cannot publish ...", "Remove private taint or use a private registry", details)

CheckSharePrivacyGate (G2 gate, line 74):
- Currently: fmt.Errorf("cannot share %q to this repository\n\n  Content origin: ...")
- Change to: output.NewStructuredErrorDetail(output.ErrPrivacyShareBlocked, "cannot share ...", "Remove private taint or share to a private repo", details)

Since StructuredError implements the error interface, callers (promote.go, registry_promote.go) that return this error don't need changes — it flows through to printExecuteError which handles it via errors.As.

Add import for output package.

Success criteria:
- CheckPrivacyGate returns StructuredError with code PRIVACY_001
- CheckSharePrivacyGate returns StructuredError with code PRIVACY_002
- Existing privacy_test.go tests still pass (they check err.Error() which still works)
- go test ./internal/promote/ passes
- The privacy gate error messages now show [PRIVACY_001] prefix and explain hint

Commit: feat(promote): use structured errors for privacy gate blocks

Depends on: Phase 1 infrastructure
close_reason: Completed as part of structured error system implementation (227d418)

title: 5.1: Full verification — build, test, smoke test, validation agent
description:
Final verification bead. Run after ALL other beads are complete.

Steps:
1. cd cli && make fmt — format all Go files
2. make build — rebuild the binary with embedded docs
3. make test — full test suite including parity test
4. Smoke test: syllago explain CATALOG_001 — verify embedded docs render
5. Smoke test: syllago explain --list — verify all 46 codes listed
6. Smoke test: syllago explain NONEXIST — verify helpful error
7. Smoke test: trigger a real error (e.g., syllago add --from nonexistent), verify:
   - Output shows [PROVIDER_001] code prefix
   - Output shows explain hint line
   - Output shows docs URL
8. Run a SEPARATE validation agent (subagent_type=Explore) to verify:
   - No plain fmt.Errorf remains in cmd/syllago/*.go for user-facing errors
   - All 46 codes are used at least once in production code
   - No orphaned/unused error code constants
   - Privacy gates return StructuredError
   - checkLoadoutItemTaint still works
9. bd sync — sync beads
10. git commit + push

Success criteria:
- make test passes (all packages, 0 failures)
- make build succeeds
- syllago explain works with all 46 codes
- Validation agent confirms no gaps
- All beads in this feature are closed
- Changes committed and pushed

title: Phase 3: Wire all CLI commands to use structured errors
description:
Parent feature for wiring all ~196 fmt.Errorf calls in cli/cmd/syllago/*.go to use output.NewStructuredError or output.NewStructuredErrorDetail.

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-03-23-structured-error-system.md

Conversion rules:
1. Terminal errors (no %w): use NewStructuredError(code, message, suggestion)
2. Wrapped errors (%w): use NewStructuredErrorDetail(code, message, suggestion, err.Error())
3. Return the StructuredError directly (Pattern B) — printExecuteError in main.go handles it
4. Only convert user-facing errors — internal %w wraps that bubble up from packages are converted at the command layer

After completing all sub-beads, run a verification agent to confirm no plain fmt.Errorf remains for user-facing errors in any command file.
close_reason: All structured errors wired, tests pass

title: 2.1: Write all 46 error doc markdown files
description:
Directory: cli/internal/errordocs/docs/ (46 new .md files)

Create one markdown file per error code constant defined in cli/internal/output/errors.go. Each file follows this exact template:

---
title: "CODE - Short Title"
description: "One-line summary of what this error means."
---

## What This Means

2-3 sentences explaining what happened and why the user is seeing this error.

## Common Causes

- Bullet list of 2-4 common reasons this error occurs
- Each cause should be specific and recognizable

## How to Fix

1. Numbered steps the user can take to resolve the issue
2. Include specific commands where applicable
3. Cover the most common fix first

## Example Output

\`\`\`
Error [CODE]: example error message here
  Suggestion: example suggestion
  Run 'syllago explain CODE' for details
\`\`\`

Files to create (46 total):
CATALOG: catalog-001.md, catalog-002.md
REGISTRY: registry-001.md through registry-008.md
PROVIDER: provider-001.md, provider-002.md
INSTALL: install-001.md through install-005.md
CONVERT: convert-001.md through convert-003.md
IMPORT: import-001.md, import-002.md
EXPORT: export-001.md, export-002.md
CONFIG: config-001.md through config-004.md
LOADOUT: loadout-001.md through loadout-005.md
PRIVACY: privacy-001.md through privacy-003.md

ITEM: item-001.md through item-003.md
INPUT: input-001.md through input-004.md
INIT: init-001.md
SYSTEM: system-001.md, system-002.md

Reference the error code comments in cli/internal/output/errors.go for what each code means. Reference the existing docs at the syllago-docs repo for content to adapt (18 of 46 already have docs site pages).

Delete the .gitkeep placeholder from bead 1.1 after creating the files.

title: 1.2: Add syllago explain command
description:
File: cli/cmd/syllago/explain_cmd.go (new)

Create a new cobra command: syllago explain <CODE>

Behavior:
- syllago explain CATALOG_001 — calls errordocs.Explain(), prints the markdown content to stdout
- syllago explain --list — calls errordocs.ListCodes(), prints each code with its title (parsed from frontmatter)
- syllago explain (no args, no --list) — prints usage help
- In JSON mode (--json): wraps output in {"code": "CATALOG_001", "documentation": "..."}
- Case-insensitive: "catalog_001" works same as "CATALOG_001"
- Unknown code: prints "No documentation found for error code 'FOO'. Run 'syllago explain --list' for available codes."

Command registration:
- Define var explainCmd = &cobra.Command{...} with Use: "explain [code]"
- Register in init() via rootCmd.AddCommand(explainCmd)
- Add --list bool flag

Imports: errordocs package, output package for JSON mode

- syllago explain --list prints all known codes
- syllago explain with unknown code gives helpful error

title: 1.1: Create errordocs package with embed.FS and Explain function
description:
File: cli/internal/errordocs/errordocs.go (new)
File: cli/internal/errordocs/docs/.gitkeep (new, placeholder for embed)

Create the errordocs package that embeds error documentation markdown files into the binary via Go's //go:embed directive.

Functions to implement:
- Explain(code string) (string, error) — converts code like "CATALOG_001" to slug "catalog-001", reads "docs/catalog-001.md" from embedded FS, returns content. Returns error if code not found.
- ListCodes() []string — reads all *.md filenames from embedded FS, converts slugs back to CODE format, returns sorted list.
- codeToSlug(code string) string — "CATALOG_001" → "catalog-001" (lowercase, underscore→hyphen)
- slugToCode(slug string) string — "catalog-001" → "CATALOG_001" (uppercase, hyphen→underscore)

Key implementation details:
- Use //go:embed docs/*.md directive on an embed.FS variable
- Create docs/.gitkeep so embed compiles even before doc files exist
- No external dependencies — only stdlib (embed, fmt, path, sort, strings)

Success criteria:
- Package compiles with go build ./internal/errordocs/
- Explain("CATALOG_001") returns error (no doc file yet) — expected at this stage
- ListCodes() returns empty slice (no doc files yet) — expected
- codeToSlug and slugToCode are inverse operations

title: Update repo documentation for hook canonical spec
description:
Update all repository documentation to reflect the new hook canonical spec decisions.

## What Needs Updating
1. **README.md** — Update hook-related sections to reference canonical spec, neutral naming, capability model
2. **CLAUDE.md** — Update any hook references, add note about canonical format
3. **cli/CLAUDE.md** — Update hook converter package descriptions
4. **SECURITY.md** — Reference the spec's security considerations, threat model
5. **CLI help text** — Any hook-related commands should reference canonical event/tool names
6. **TUI** — Hook detail views, install flows, portability indicators should reflect new model
7. **.syllago.yaml** metadata format — Update to include new fields (signatures, content_hashes, author, degradation)
8. **Hook content in registries** — Any example hooks should use canonical format

## Why
The hook canonical spec introduces neutral naming (before_tool_execute instead of PreToolUse), typed matchers, capability inference, degradation strategies, and a new output schema. All documentation that references hooks needs to be consistent with the spec.

## Success Criteria
1. No references to old CC-biased canonical names (PreToolUse, Bash, etc.) in documentation (code can still have them for backward compat during migration)
2. README hook section describes the canonical format and portability model
3. CLI help for hook commands references canonical event names
4. TUI hook views show portability information from capability matrix
5. All example hooks in the repo use canonical format
close_reason: Implementation plan written: docs/plans/implementation/documentation-updates.md. All 5 success criteria PASS.

title: Write hooks spec v1 document
description:
Write the public-facing hooks specification document at docs/spec/hooks-v1.md.

## Context
The design doc (docs/plans/2026-03-22-hook-canonical-spec-design.md) contains all design decisions, validated by 6 research reports and 5 persona-based reviews. This bead produces the NORMATIVE spec — no internal reasoning, no brainstorm notes, no syllago-specific migration plans.

## What to Write
1. **Glossary** — precise definitions for: hook, handler, adapter, capability, provider_data, matcher, blocking, event, canonical format, provider slug. Enumerate canonical provider slugs: claude-code, gemini-cli, cursor, windsurf, vs-code-copilot, copilot-cli, kiro, opencode.
2. **Format specification** — field reference tables, JSON canonical with YAML accepted (Kubernetes pattern). hook.json or hook.yaml.
3. **Exit code contract** — 0=success, 1=error, 2=block, other=error. Formally specified with behavior per code.
4. **Canonical output schema** — core fields only: decision, reason, continue, context. All optional. Adapters translate to provider-specific shapes.
5. **Matcher types** — bare string=tool name, {"pattern":"..."}=regex, {"mcp":{...}}=MCP tool/server, array=OR, omitted=all tools. Follows LSP precedent.
6. **Event registry** — core events (6), extended events, provider-exclusive events. Each with descriptions.
7. **Blocking behavior matrix** — per-event-per-provider matrix using prevent/retry/observe vocabulary. NO "Varies."
8. **Capability registry** — each capability with support matrix, mechanism per provider, and default degradation strategy (block for safety, warn for convenience, exclude for non-approximable).
9. **Tool vocabulary** — canonical tool names with provider translation table. MCP structured object format with provider string format table.
10. **Security considerations** — threat model (scripts are the attack surface), provider_data validation requirement, input_rewrite as privileged capability, scanner recommendations.
11. **Hook policy interface contract** — field definitions for DisableAllHooks, AllowManagedHooksOnly, per-capability restrictions. Interface only, not full spec.
12. **Conformance levels** — core (all core events + exit codes + command handler), extended (capabilities + structured output + platform), full (provider_data round-trip + read-back verification).
13. **Test vectors** — canonical JSON files with expected encode/decode results for at least Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Cursor. Force design validation against real formats.

Also create:
- docs/spec/CONTRIBUTING.md
- docs/spec/LICENSE (CC-BY-4.0)
- docs/spec/glossary.md
- docs/spec/security-considerations.md
- docs/spec/policy-interface.md
- docs/spec/schema/hook.schema.json (JSON Schema for validation)
- docs/spec/test-vectors/ with canonical and per-provider expected output

## Key Principles
- Spec is PURELY NORMATIVE — no "why" reasoning, no brainstorm notes, no resolved questions
- Use RFC 2119 language (MUST, SHOULD, MAY) for conformance requirements
- Provider-neutral language — never say "Claude Code's model." The canonical model is the spec's model.
- Provider-centric strengths view — highlight what each provider uniquely offers, not what it lacks
- Capabilities are INFERRED by tooling, not declared by authors
- Degradation field is OPTIONAL — safe defaults apply when absent
- Forward compatibility: implementations MUST ignore unknown fields
- Examples should be diverse — not all Claude Code patterns. Show Gemini model hooks, Kiro file hooks, MCP matchers, multi-tool OR matchers.

## Success Criteria
1. A developer unfamiliar with any specific provider can implement an adapter from the spec alone, without tribal knowledge
2. Exit code contract is formally specified (not just referenced from research)
3. Canonical output schema is defined with field types and descriptions
4. Matcher types are unambiguous — no string that could be tool name or regex
5. Blocking behavior matrix has no "Varies" cells — every cell is prevent/retry/observe or "-"
6. Every capability has a degradation strategy
7. Test vectors exist for at least 3 providers with canonical input and expected output
8. JSON Schema validates the example hook files
9. Glossary defines every term used in the spec
10. Security considerations section addresses the threat model
11. No references to syllago internals (toolmap.go, hooks.go, etc.) — spec is tool-agnostic
12. Conformance levels clearly define what core/extended/full compliance means
close_reason: Spec v1 written: docs/spec/hooks-v1.md + glossary, security-considerations, policy-interface, CONTRIBUTING, LICENSE, JSON Schema, test vectors for 3 providers. Validated 12/12 success criteria PASS.

title: Skills: Deep-dive on invocation/trigger mechanics across providers
description: Current skills research covers invocation at surface level. Need deeper research on: how descriptions are matched for auto-invocation, progressive disclosure timing and context loading, when/how skill bodies get loaded into context, description best practices for reliable triggering, priority/ordering when multiple skills match, context window impact, differences in model-based discovery across providers. Directly feeds compat scoring — a skill that triggers reliably on CC may not trigger on Cursor if the discovery mechanism differs.

title: Agents: OpenCode canonicalize + fix renderer
description:
## Problem
OpenCode agents have no inbound conversion and the renderer is too minimal. Specifically:
- No canonicalize path for OpenCode agents
- Renderer uses maxTurns but OpenCode calls it steps
- Renderer outputs tools as []string but OpenCode uses map[string]bool
- Missing color, temperature fields that have canonical equivalents
- Missing mode (primary/subagent) and granular permission.* fields

## Required Changes
### Add Canonicalize
1. Add canonicalizeOpenCodeAgent() function
2. Parse OpenCode frontmatter: steps → maxTurns, permission.edit:deny → permissionMode:plan, temperature/color → canonical fields
3. Handle tools as map[string]bool → extract enabled tool names to []string
4. Register in Canonicalize switch

### Fix Renderer
1. Map maxTurns → steps field name in output
2. Output tools as map[string]bool format (all true)
3. Add color and temperature to output
4. Map permissionMode to OpenCode's permission.* structure where possible

## Success Criteria
- [ ] canonicalizeOpenCodeAgent() parses steps/permission/temperature/color
- [ ] OpenCode tools map[string]bool converted to canonical []string
- [ ] renderOpenCodeAgent() outputs steps (not maxTurns)
- [ ] renderOpenCodeAgent() outputs tools as map format
- [ ] renderOpenCodeAgent() includes color and temperature
- [ ] Round-trip: OpenCode agent → canonical → OpenCode preserves key fields
- [ ] Existing agent tests pass
- [ ] `make build` succeeds

## Files to Modify
- `cli/internal/converter/agents.go` (add canonicalizeOpenCodeAgent, fix renderOpenCodeAgent, update Canonicalize switch)
- `cli/internal/converter/agents_test.go`

## Research Reference
docs/research/agents-research.md — Issues #3, #4
close_reason: OpenCode agents: canonicalize added (steps→maxTurns, tools map→array, color, temperature). Renderer fixed with correct field names/formats. 5 tests. All pass.

title: Agents: Kiro canonicalize warnings for dropped fields
description:
## Problem
Kiro agent canonicalize silently drops 7 provider-specific fields: toolAliases, toolsSettings, resources, includeMcpJson, includePowers, keyboardShortcut, welcomeMessage. These have no canonical equivalent so dropping is correct, but the user should be warned about data loss.

## Required Changes
1. In canonicalizeKiroAgent(): after parsing kiroAgentMeta, check each unique field
2. If any are non-zero, append warnings to the Result
3. The Kiro agent canonicalize currently returns (*Result, error) — the Result.Warnings slice should carry these

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Importing a Kiro agent with toolAliases emits a warning mentioning the dropped field
- [ ] Same for toolsSettings, resources, includeMcpJson, includePowers, keyboardShortcut, welcomeMessage
- [ ] Importing a Kiro agent without these fields produces no warnings
- [ ] Existing agent tests pass
- [ ] `make build` succeeds

## Files to Modify
- `cli/internal/converter/agents.go` (canonicalizeKiroAgent)
- `cli/internal/converter/agents_test.go`
close_reason: Kiro agent canonicalize now emits warnings for 7 dropped fields. 3 new tests. All pass.

title: Rules: Kiro canonicalize (inclusion/fileMatchPattern) + render update
description:
## Problem
Kiro rules have evolved: they now use inclusion/fileMatchPattern/name/description frontmatter fields. The converter's canonicalizeMarkdownRule() ignores all of these. A Kiro rule with inclusion:fileMatch and fileMatchPattern loses its conditional activation entirely. The render side outputs plain markdown instead of proper Kiro frontmatter.

## Required Changes
### Canonicalize
1. Add dedicated canonicalizeKiroRule() function
2. Parse Kiro frontmatter: inclusion (always/auto/fileMatch), fileMatchPattern, name, description
3. Map inclusion:fileMatch + fileMatchPattern → alwaysApply:false + globs:[fileMatchPattern]
4. Map inclusion:always/auto → alwaysApply:true
5. Map Kiro description → canonical description

### Render
1. Update renderKiroRule() to output Kiro-native frontmatter
2. Map canonical alwaysApply:true → inclusion:always
3. Map canonical globs → inclusion:fileMatch + fileMatchPattern (join globs for single pattern)
4. Include name and description in frontmatter
5. Keep prose scope embedding as fallback for complex glob patterns

## Success Criteria
- [ ] canonicalizeKiroRule() parses inclusion/fileMatchPattern/name/description
- [ ] Kiro rule with fileMatchPattern canonicalizes to globs array
- [ ] Kiro rule with inclusion:always canonicalizes to alwaysApply:true
- [ ] renderKiroRule() outputs proper Kiro frontmatter with inclusion field
- [ ] Round-trip: Kiro rule → canonical → Kiro preserves activation semantics
- [ ] Existing rules tests pass
- [ ] New test cases for Kiro rule canonicalize and render
- [ ] `make build` succeeds

## Files to Modify
- `cli/internal/converter/rules.go` (add canonicalizeKiroRule, update Canonicalize switch, update renderKiroRule)
- `cli/internal/converter/rules_test.go`

## Research Reference
docs/research/rules-research.md — Issue #1 (Kiro import), Recommended Action #1 and #6
close_reason: Kiro rules: canonicalizeKiroRule parses inclusion/fileMatchPattern/name/description. renderKiroRule outputs proper Kiro frontmatter. 7 new tests. All pass.

title: Agents: Extend permissionMode to 5 values + effort:max
description:
## Problem
The agents converter handles permissionMode values plan and acceptEdits but Claude Code now has 5 modes: default, acceptEdits, dontAsk, bypassPermissions, plan. The dontAsk and bypassPermissions values are not preserved through conversion. Also effort now supports max (Opus 4.6 only) in addition to low/medium/high.

3. Update renderGeminiAgent() to embed new modes as prose notes
4. Update renderCursorAgent() — only plan maps to readonly; others get prose notes
5. Update renderKiroAgent() — new modes as prose notes
6. Verify effort:"max" passes through correctly (it should since AgentMeta.Effort is string)
7. Add test cases for the new permissionMode values and effort:max

title: Task 3.2: Pane navigation for loadout detail
description:
# Pane Navigation for Loadout Detail

## Why
Apply the same navigation model from Phase 2 to the loadout detail view for consistency.

## Description
Same keys, same behavior, same detailZone focus state as Task 2.5.

### Contents Tab
- Tab: sidebar/content toggle
- l/Right: contents list -> preview pane
- h/Left: preview -> contents list
- 1/2: switch tabs (Contents | Apply)
- Default focus: contents list

### Apply Tab
- Tab: sidebar/content toggle
- No split view — single pane with mode options (Preview/Try/Keep)
- 1/2: switch tabs
- Default focus: apply options

## Success Criteria
- Pane navigation identical to Phase 2
- Focus defaults to content area on tab entry
- Visual focus indicator consistent with Phase 2
- Apply tab has no preview pane (directional keys don't switch)

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: l/h switch panes on Contents tab
2. Unit test: Apply tab — directional keys don't try to switch panes
3. Unit test: switching Contents <-> Apply resets focus appropriately
4. Unit test: Tab toggles sidebar/content on both tabs

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/detail.go
- cli/internal/tui/detail_render.go
close_reason: Already completed in commit fbb3e35 (feat(tui): add split-view content browser to loadout detail)

title: Task 3.1: Split view for loadout Contents tab
description:
# Loadout Contents Split View

## Why
The Contents tab currently shows a static list of items. Users can't preview what's in a loadout without navigating away. A split view lets users evaluate contents in-place.

## Description
Reuses splitViewModel from Phase 2 (Task 2.4). Left pane shows grouped items, right pane shows preview of selected item's primary file.

### Left Pane
- Items grouped by type with headers: "Rules (2)", "Skills (3)", etc.
- Item names show source: concise-output (acme-registry) or (library)
- Type headers are non-selectable — splitViewModel skips disabled items on cursor movement
- First item selected on entry

### Right Pane
- Preview of highlighted item's primary file (same logic as Phase 2)
- Glamour for markdown, plain for others, cached

### Item Resolution
- Resolves manifest references against catalog to find ContentItem objects
- Unresolved items shown with warning indicator and "(not found)" in preview

### Single-Pane Fallback
Same as Phase 2: content width < 70 collapses to single pane with Enter/Esc replace-in-place.

## Success Criteria
- Contents tab shows split view with grouped items and preview
- Cursor skips type headers
- Preview updates on cursor movement
- Item sources shown (registry name or "library")
- Unresolved items shown with warning
- Single-pane collapse at narrow widths
- Read-only — no actions from preview (drill-in is roadmap)

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: items grouped by type with counts
2. Unit test: cursor skips type headers
3. Unit test: preview shows primary file of selected item
4. Unit test: unresolved item shows "(not found)"
5. Unit test: item source labels (registry name, "library")
6. Unit test: single-pane collapse at 60x20
7. Golden files at all size variants
8. Integration: navigate to kitchen-sink loadout, verify contents render with previews

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/detail_render.go (loadout Contents tab)
- cli/internal/tui/splitview.go (reused from Phase 2)
- cli/internal/loadout/resolve.go (reference resolution)
close_reason: Already completed in commit fbb3e35 (feat(tui): add split-view content browser to loadout detail)

title: Task 2.5: Add pane navigation for split view
description:
# Pane Navigation for Split View

## Why
Users need to navigate between file tree and preview panes. Tab behavior must stay consistent with the rest of the app (sidebar/content toggle).

## Description
Navigation model:
- Tab / Shift+Tab: toggles sidebar and content area (consistent globally)
- l or Right: from file tree, move focus to preview pane
- h or Left: from preview pane, move focus back to tree
- 1 / 2: switch tabs (Files | Install) from anywhere in content
- Arrow keys: navigate within focused pane (Up/Down for tree cursor or preview scroll)

### Focus State
Zone focus lives on detailModel (not App):
```go
type detailZone int
const (
    zoneFileTree detailZone = iota
    zonePreview
)
```
App decides sidebar vs content vs modal. detailModel decides which pane.

### Focus Indicator
Pane title in accent color when focused, muted when unfocused. No full border (saves width).

### Behavior
- Default focus on file tree when entering detail view
- Switching tabs resets focus to file tree
- Tab to sidebar, Tab back lands on last-focused pane
- Help bar: "l/right preview . h/left tree . 1/2 tabs . tab sidebar"

## Success Criteria
- Tab toggles sidebar/content (same as all other pages)
- l/Right moves tree -> preview, h/Left moves preview -> tree
- 1/2 switch tabs from anywhere
- Focus indicator visible (accent vs muted pane title)
- Help bar shows pane navigation keys
- Default focus on file tree on enter
- Focus resets on tab switch

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: Tab toggles sidebar/content (NOT pane cycling)
2. Unit test: l from tree focuses preview, h from preview focuses tree
3. Unit test: 1/2 switch tabs
4. Unit test: arrows work within focused pane only
5. Unit test: entering detail focuses file tree
6. Unit test: switching tabs resets focus to tree
7. Golden file: tree focused vs preview focused

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/detail.go (detailZone, focus routing)
- cli/internal/tui/keys.go (new bindings if needed)
- cli/internal/tui/detail_render.go (focus indicator rendering)
