title: Task 2.4: Add split-view to Files tab with reusable splitViewModel
description:
# Split-View Files Tab

## Why
With Overview tab removed, the Files tab becomes the primary view. A split layout (file tree + preview) lets users see file contents without extra navigation. The splitViewModel is designed as a reusable component for Phase 3.

## Description
Transform the Files tab into a split-view layout.

### Component Design
Build as a standalone splitViewModel:
- Left pane: file tree with cursor, scroll
- Right pane: preview content with independent scroll
- focusedPane field (0=left, 1=right)
- collapsed bool for narrow terminals
- showingPreview bool for single-pane mode

### Layout
- Left pane: 35% width (min 25 chars)
- Right pane: 65% (remaining)
- Separator: | character
- Activation: content width >= 70 (not terminal width)
- Adaptive ratio: 40/60 at content widths 70-90, 35/65 at 100+

### Single-Pane Fallback (content width < 70)
Replace-in-place interaction:
- Mode 1 (file tree): full-width tree, Enter opens file
- Mode 2 (file content): full-width content replaces tree, Esc returns to tree with cursor preserved

### Primary File by Type
Skills -> SKILL.md, Agents -> .md file, Rules -> rule .md, Hooks -> config, MCP -> config, Commands -> command file, Loadouts -> loadout.yaml

### File Tree
- Directories always expanded (no collapse toggle)
- 2 spaces indentation per nesting level
- Built from ContentItem.Files []string

### Preview
- Glamour render for markdown, plain text for others
- Render cache keyed by file path
- First 200 lines for large files with "(N more lines)" indicator
- Binary files: "(binary file)" placeholder
- Width-constrained glamour style matching pane width

## Success Criteria
- Split view at content width >= 70
- Single-pane replace-in-place at content width < 70
- Primary file pre-selected and previewed on entry
- File tree navigation updates preview in real-time
- Preview scrolls independently when focused
- Glamour cache prevents re-renders on revisit
- Works for all content types

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: split view renders at 120x40
2. Unit test: single-pane file tree at 60x20
3. Unit test: Enter opens file in single-pane, Esc returns with cursor preserved
4. Unit test: primary file selection for each content type
5. Unit test: cursor navigation updates preview content
6. Unit test: independent preview scroll
7. Unit test: glamour cache hit on revisit
8. Unit test: 200-line cap for large files
9. Golden files at all size variants
10. Boundary: 50+ files (tree scroll)

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/splitview.go (NEW — reusable component)
- cli/internal/tui/detail.go
- cli/internal/tui/detail_render.go
close_reason: Already completed in earlier commits (86cb395, f6b7935)

title: Task 2.1: Remove README.md infrastructure from codebase
description:
# Remove README.md Infrastructure

## Why
README.md is an unnecessary layer of indirection. Skills have SKILL.md, agents are markdown files, hooks are configs — the content IS the documentation. Removing README simplifies authoring and reduces code complexity (parsing, rendering, warnings for missing README).

## Description
Remove all README.md handling from the codebase:

- catalog/scanner.go: Remove loadReadme(), readDescription() calls for README.md, README.md warnings, ReadmeBody field population
- catalog/types.go: Remove ReadmeBody field from ContentItem
- cli/internal/readme/: Delete entire package
- catalog/scanner.go: Update description extraction to use primary content files (SKILL.md frontmatter, first paragraph of agent .md, etc.)
- scanner.go:362: Remove README.md from isMetaFile() skip list — README.md becomes a regular file in the file tree

Note: Removing README.md from isMetaFile() means any README.md files in user-authored content directories will start appearing in the file tree. Call out in PR description.

## Success Criteria
- ReadmeBody field removed from ContentItem struct
- readme package deleted
- No code references README.md as a special file
- Content descriptions still populated from primary content files
- make test passes (after updating dependent tests)

## Test-Driven Development

2. Unit test: description extracted from SKILL.md frontmatter description field
3. Unit test: agent description extracted from first paragraph of .md file
4. Unit test: hook description extracted from config file

## Key Files

- cli/internal/catalog/types.go

title: Task 1.6: Add JSON validity check for settings file merges
description:
# Add JSON Validity Check for Settings File Merges

## Why
readJSONFileOrEmpty() doesn't validate that the file contains valid JSON before sjson operates on it. If settings.json is malformed (truncated, hand-edited with syntax error), sjson silently produces malformed output or returns a cryptic error. The snapshot protects against data loss but the error path is confusing.

## Description
Add json.Valid() check after reading the settings file in readJSONFileOrEmpty(). If invalid, return a clear error: "settings.json contains invalid JSON; fix or delete the file before applying a loadout."

## Success Criteria
- readJSONFileOrEmpty() checks json.Valid() before returning file contents
- Malformed JSON returns clear, actionable error message
- Valid JSON files continue to work as before
- Missing files still return "{}" (no change to that behavior)

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: valid JSON file returns contents
2. Unit test: missing file returns "{}"
3. Unit test: malformed JSON returns error with descriptive message
4. Unit test: empty file returns error (not valid JSON)
5. Unit test: truncated JSON returns error

## Key Files
- cli/internal/loadout/apply.go (readJSONFileOrEmpty, ~line 463)
close_reason: Added json.Valid() check to readJSONFileOrEmpty() with clear error message. 5 unit tests covering valid, missing, malformed, empty, and truncated JSON.

title: Task 1.4: Add review step with security callouts
description:
# Review Step Before Creation

## Why
Users need to see exactly what they're about to create before committing. This is especially important for security — hooks and MCP configs define executable commands that will run on the user's system.

## Description
After destination selection and before committing, show a full summary grouped by content type. The user can confirm to create or go back to edit.

### Security Callout
If the loadout includes hooks or MCP configs (especially from registries), the review step explicitly displays commands that will be executed:

```
!! Security Notice !!
This loadout includes executable content:
  Hook: PostToolUse -> "./scripts/lint.sh"
  MCP:  my-server   -> "node server.js --port 3000"
Review these commands before installing.
Trust this code and verify it before putting it in your environment.
```

### Item Display
- Names include source in parentheses when ambiguous: concise-output (acme-registry)
- Types with >3 names that overflow modal width truncate with "+ N more"
- Empty types omitted from summary

### Completion Signal
Add confirmed bool field to modal. Create sets confirmed=true + active=false. Esc sets only active=false. App checks confirmed to distinguish completion from cancellation.

## Success Criteria
- All metadata and selections displayed in readable grouped format
- Hook/MCP commands displayed with security warning when present
- Item names show source qualifier when ambiguous across registries
- Long item lists truncated with "+ N more"
- Back returns to destination step, Create triggers doCreateLoadout()
- confirmed field correctly distinguishes completion from cancellation
- Review step scrolls if content exceeds modal height, buttons pinned to bottom
- Mouse: clickable buttons

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: review renders correct summary for mixed selection
2. Unit test: review with hooks shows security warning with command strings
3. Unit test: review with MCP shows server commands
4. Unit test: review with 0 items shows warning
5. Unit test: Back returns to dest step with state preserved
6. Unit test: Esc sets confirmed=false, Create sets confirmed=true
7. Unit test: long item list truncates with "+ N more"
8. Unit test: ambiguous names show source qualifier
9. Golden file: review step with mixed content types (80x30)
10. Golden file: review step with hooks/MCP security warning

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/loadout_create.go (new review step)
- cli/internal/tui/app.go (check confirmed field)
close_reason: Review step enhanced: actual hook/MCP commands in security callout, ambiguous name disambiguation, +N more truncation for long type lists, scroll support with Up/Down keys. 9 new tests covering all requirements.

title: Task 1.3: Replace flat item list with per-type item selection
description:
# Per-Type Item Selection Steps

## Why
A single flat list of all catalog items doesn't scale and doesn't help users think about what they're building. Walking through each content type individually matches the mental model and supports provider-specific filtering.

## Description
Each selected content type gets its own wizard step with a checkbox list of items.

### Entry Filtering
When the provider is selected, build a map[ContentType][]loadoutItemEntry that pre-filters all catalog items by type and provider compatibility. This map is reused across all steps. The t key toggles "show all" mode where incompatible items appear grayed out (muted + strikethrough + "(incompatible)" suffix).

### Per-Type State
Each type step preserves its own cursor position, scroll offset, and search query in maps (map[ContentType]int). Navigating back restores exact position. No state is cleared on Esc — only step and typeStepIndex change.

### Step Tracking
Composite approach: existing step enum + typeStepIndex int + selectedTypes []ContentType. When step == clStepItems, typeStepIndex indicates which type is active. Display: fixedStepsBefore + typeStepIndex + 1 of fixedSteps + len(selectedTypes).

### Empty Types
If a selected type has 0 available items, show the step with "No {type} available for {provider}" and require Enter to proceed. Do NOT auto-advance.

### Keys
- Space: toggle selection
- a: Select All / Deselect All
- t: toggle compatibility filter (show all vs compatible only)
- /: search within current type
- Enter: next type step
- Esc: previous type step (or type selection if on first type)

## Success Criteria
- Each content type gets its own step with pre-filtered items
- t toggles compatible-only vs show-all modes, current mode visible in help text
- Incompatible items use strikethrough + "(incompatible)" — not just color
- Search filters within current type, selections persist after clearing
- Back-navigation preserves selections, cursor, and scroll offset from all previous steps
- Empty types show message, require Enter
- a key selects/deselects all visible items
- Mouse: clickable checkboxes and items

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: Rules step shows 3 compatible items, Space toggles
2. Unit test: t key reveals incompatible items grayed out with strikethrough
3. Unit test: a key selects/deselects all
4. Unit test: search reduces visible items, selections persist after clearing
5. Unit test: Esc from first type -> type selection, all selections preserved
6. Unit test: Esc from Rules -> advance back to Rules -> cursor position preserved
7. Unit test: empty type step shows message, Enter advances, Esc goes back
8. Unit test: per-type scroll offset preserved on back-nav
9. Integration test: full flow through 3 types, all selections in manifest
10. Golden file: per-type item selection step (80x30)

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/loadout_create.go
- cli/internal/catalog/types.go (ContentType)
- cli/internal/provider/provider.go (SupportsType)
close_reason: Implementation complete from prior sessions. Added 11 missing TDD tests (toggle-all, incompatible rendering, search persistence, back-nav cursor/scroll preservation, empty type handling, full flow integration) plus empty-type rendering code path.

title: Task 1.2: Add content type selection step to wizard
description:
# Add Content Type Selection Step

## Why
Before walking through individual content types, users should select which types they want in their loadout. This reduces friction — most users want all types (default checked), power users can uncheck types they don't need.

## Description
New step inserted between provider selection and per-type item selection. Shows checkboxes for each content type supported by the selected provider, defaulting all to checked. Only types with available items in the catalog are shown.

Microcopy: "Uncheck any content types you want to skip."

Keys:
- Space: toggle individual checkbox
- a: Select All / Deselect All toggle
- Enter: proceed to first per-type item step
- Esc: go back to provider step

Mouse: clicking checkbox or type label toggles it.

## Success Criteria
- After selecting a provider, checkbox screen shows all supported content types with available items, all checked by default
- Space toggles individual, a toggles all
- Enter proceeds, Esc goes back
- Selecting 0 types + Enter shows "Select at least one content type"
- Mouse clicks toggle checkboxes
- Modal fits at 60x20 terminal (height capped to min(24, termHeight-2))

## Test-Driven Development

3. Unit test: 0 types + Enter shows validation error
4. Unit test: Space toggles individual type
5. Unit test: Esc goes back to provider step
6. Unit test: mouse click on checkbox toggles it

title: Task 1.0: Add shared isValidName() for all user-entered names
description:
# Add Shared Name Validation

## Why
User-entered names become directory names via filepath.Join(destDir, name) with no character validation. Names like ../../.ssh/foo create directories outside the intended tree. This is a path traversal vulnerability that must be fixed before building the new wizard on top.

## Description
Add a shared isValidName() function used everywhere: loadout names, import names, content names. One rule, one validator, no gaps.

Restriction: [a-zA-Z0-9_-], no dots, no path separators, no leading dash, max 100 chars.

Validate in both:
- Wizard UI (immediate feedback at the name input step)
- doCreateLoadout() and manifest Parse() (defense-in-depth)

Also fix: home, _ := os.UserHomeDir() in doCreateLoadout() — check the error.

## Success Criteria
- isValidName() function exists and is used for all user-entered names
- Names with path separators, dots, or special chars are rejected with clear error message
- Existing code in doCreateLoadout(), import, and manifest.Parse() calls isValidName()
- os.UserHomeDir() error is checked in doCreateLoadout()

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: isValidName("my-loadout") returns true
2. Unit test: isValidName("../../.ssh/foo") returns false
3. Unit test: isValidName("../evil") returns false
4. Unit test: isValidName(".hidden") returns false
5. Unit test: isValidName("") returns false
6. Unit test: isValidName with 101 chars returns false
7. Unit test: wizard name step shows error for invalid names

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/app.go (doCreateLoadout)
- cli/internal/loadout/manifest.go (Parse)
- New: shared validation function (location TBD — config or model package)
close_reason: Implemented shared name validation with IsValidItemName (enhanced with leading-dash reject + 100-char max) and ValidateUserName (human-readable errors). Applied at UI layer (import wizard, create loadout wizard) and defense-in-depth (doCreateLoadoutFromScreen, manifest.Parse). Full TDD with unit and integration tests.

title: Task 1.1: Diagnose and fix loadout creation bug
description:
# Diagnose and Fix Loadout Creation Bug

## Why
Loadouts created via the wizard don't appear in the TUI after creation. This is the most basic prerequisite — the feature is broken.

## Description
The doCreateLoadoutMsg handler (app.go:835) rescans the catalog and refreshes sidebar counts, but the loadout still doesn't show. Possible causes:
- Catalog scanner not finding the new loadout directory
- Path mismatch (writing to a location the scanner doesn't search)
- Sidebar/card refresh issue after catalog rescan

## Success Criteria
- Create a loadout via the wizard -> it appears immediately in the Loadouts card view
- Sidebar count updates to reflect the new loadout
- Works for both Project and Library destinations
- Toast shows success message with loadout name

## Test-Driven Development
Write tests FIRST, then fix:
1. Unit test: create loadout via doCreateLoadout() with a temp catalog, verify doCreateLoadoutMsg triggers catalog rescan that includes the new loadout
2. Integration test: full wizard flow (provider -> items -> name -> dest -> confirm) -> verify loadout appears in rendered output
3. Verify golden files for loadout cards view still pass

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/app.go (lines 345-410, 835-846)
- cli/internal/tui/loadout_create.go
- cli/internal/catalog/scanner.go
close_reason: Bug diagnosed and fixed. The handler code at app.go:932 was already correct (rescan + navigate to detail). Fixed Library destination path to use catalog.GlobalContentDir() instead of hardcoded os.UserHomeDir() path (single source of truth). Added 2 integration tests: TestCreateLoadoutHandlerNavigatesToDetail (full handler flow verifying detail nav, toast, sidebar count) and TestCreateLoadoutLibraryDestination (Library dest via GlobalContentDirOverride). All tests pass.

title: Provider discovery: D/select-all/add-selected buttons broken
description:
## Bug
On the "Add provider" discovery screen (stepDiscoverySelect), multiple controls are broken when the item list is large (e.g., Claude provider with many items):
- D key only toggles the current cursor line instead of deselecting all
- "Select all" only toggles the current line
- "Add selected" only toggles the current line
- All three buttons appear to do the same thing (toggle current item)
- No scrolling when the list exceeds terminal height
- Click targets are misaligned

**Note:** With smaller lists (e.g., Gemini with few items), the buttons work correctly. This suggests the bug is triggered by list overflow — likely the click zones / button zones are calculated wrong when content scrolls or overflows.

## Additional UX Issues (same flow)
- No confirmation modal before adding items ("Are you sure you want to add N items?")
- This is both a best practice and accessibility requirement

## Files
- `cli/internal/tui/import.go` — stepDiscoverySelect handling, updateDiscoverySelect(), View() for stepDiscoverySelect

## Impact
Users cannot import multiple discovered items at once when the list is large. The entire discovery multi-select flow is non-functional for providers with many items.
close_reason: Added scroll windowing to discovery select list, Home/End keybindings, and mouse wheel support

title: Fix data races in registry add and remove
description:
## Phase 0a: Fix data races in registry add and remove

### Problem
Both doRegistryAdd (app.go:216-258) and doRegistryRemove (app.go:191-205) capture `cfg := a.registryCfg` (a pointer) then mutate `cfg.Registries` in a goroutine. Since cfg points to a.registryCfg, View() can read the slice concurrently — data race.

### Implementation
**Strategy:** Inside each command closure, load a fresh config from disk, modify the fresh copy, save. Never touch a.registryCfg from the goroutine.

**Remove (app.go:191-205):**
1. Pass only the registry name (string) into the closure — not the cfg pointer
2. Inside closure: `freshCfg, err := config.Load(repoRoot)`
3. Filter out the registry name from freshCfg.Registries
4. Save freshCfg
5. Do filesystem removal (registry.Remove)
6. If config.Load fails, return registryRemoveDoneMsg with the error

**Add (app.go:216-258):**
1. After cloning and scanning, load fresh config inside the closure
2. Append the new registry entry to the fresh config's Registries slice
3. Save the fresh config
4. Return registryAddDoneMsg as before
5. If config.Load fails, return registryAddDoneMsg with the error

**Concurrency note:** registryOpInProgress prevents concurrent registry ops from UI, so no TOCTOU risk between Load and Save.

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` lines 191-205 (remove) and 216-258 (add)

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Remove closure does NOT capture or reference `cfg` or `a.registryCfg` — only passes registry name as string
- [ ] Add closure does NOT mutate `a.registryCfg.Registries` — loads fresh config inside closure
- [ ] Both closures call config.Load() inside the goroutine, modify the fresh copy, and save
- [ ] config.Load failure returns done message with error (not silently swallowed)
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes
- [ ] No `go vet` race warnings (existing tests should cover this)

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not modify done-message handlers (those are Phase 1d)
- Do not change the message types or their fields
close_reason: Closed

title: D16: capabilities.json data_quality block + 'syllago info' footer
description:
Surface data freshness so readers of the docs site know when capability data was last refreshed.

## What to add

1. **capabilities.json schema:** add an optional top-level data_quality block with at minimum generated_at (ISO timestamp) and source_count (int). Exact shape per design doc D16.

2. **syllago info CLI:** add a footer line showing the data_quality metadata, e.g.:
   Capabilities data: generated 2026-04-14, 13 providers

## Why

Docs-side components (committed in syllago-docs under phase:mvp) are already written to render gracefully whether or not data_quality exists. Once D16 lands, the docs site picks up the data on the next sync-capabilities.ts run — no further docs changes needed.

## Does NOT depend on D1

This is independent of the D1/D13/D14 chain. Can land in any order.

## References

- Design doc D16
- Plan Out-of-Scope: docs/plans/2026-04-14-provider-pages-redesign-implementation.md:2348

title: D14: Auto-file GitHub issues from capmon validation warnings
description:
When validate.go surfaces warnings (missing required field, invalid value_type, malformed example, etc.), auto-file a GitHub issue rather than failing the build.

## Why the design chose this pattern

Provider YAMLs evolve faster than release cadence. Hard-failing on validation warnings would block releases over minor data-quality issues. Auto-filing issues creates a triage backlog the team can work through async while keeping releases moving.

## What to build

1. Detect validation warnings (distinct from errors) in cli/internal/capmon/capyaml/validate.go output
2. Deduplicate against existing open issues (by stable hash of {provider, field, warning-type})
3. Create or update a GitHub issue per distinct warning via the configured provider token
4. Emit a summary to stdout so CI logs show 'N warnings filed as issues'

## Depends on

- D1 (bd: syllago-docs-0e0) — warnings need meaningful subjects
- D13 (bd: syllago-docs-5oe) — validation logic that emits the warnings

## May overlap with

- syllago-jtafb (Capability monitor pipeline, in_progress) — check whether D14 fits inside that feature or is orthogonal before starting

## References

- Design doc D14
- Plan Out-of-Scope: docs/plans/2026-04-14-provider-pages-redesign-implementation.md:2346

title: D13: Allow-list validation for new ProviderExtension fields (capmon validate.go)
description:
Enforce quality constraints on the new ProviderExtension fields (required, value_type, examples) added in D1.

## What to add

In `cli/internal/capmon/capyaml/validate.go`:

1. `value_type` must be one of a closed allow-list (e.g., 'string', 'bool', 'int', 'string | string[]', 'number', etc.). Exact list per design doc D13.
2. `example.lang` must be one of a closed allow-list of supported syntax-highlighter langs (e.g., 'yaml', 'json', 'bash', 'toml', 'markdown', etc.).
3. `sources[].section` must match a pattern (non-empty, reasonable length).

## Why

Docs-side Zod schemas (Task 1 of the docs PR) enforce SHAPE rules only (non-empty code, required lang). The VALUE allow-lists belong on the CLI side so bad data never reaches docs. Without D13, a typo in a provider YAML's `value_type` field would silently propagate to the rendered UI.

## Depends on

- D1 (bd: syllago-docs-0e0) — fields must exist before they can be validated

## References

- Design doc D13
- Plan Out-of-Scope: docs/plans/2026-04-14-provider-pages-redesign-implementation.md:2350

title: provider-pages-redesign-validation-passed-13-tasks-ready-next-user-reviews-plan-then-create-beads-and-execute
description:
## Summary

The provider-pages-redesign workflow is past validation. All Phase 1 (brainstorm) and Phase 2 (plan + quality review + parity validation) work is complete. Implementation plan is locked. The orchestrator paused before bead creation (Stage 4) because writing 13+ persistent issues to the shared bd database warrants user review of the plan first. Next session: user reviews the plan, optionally requests edits, then explicitly authorizes bead creation, then execution begins.

## Active Work

- **Stage:** beads (paused, awaiting user ack)
- **Description:** Provider pages redesign — hybrid routing (overview + per-CT pages), structured examples, sources tables, GitHub-issue-driven warnings (deferred to CLI repo). Atomic single-PR migration per D17.
- **Progress:** Design doc final (17 decisions, 0 open Q's). Implementation plan written (2,352 lines, 13 tasks), Round-1 quality review found 6 issues (1 blocking sidebar, 3 major, 2 minor) — all fixed. Round-2 quality review PASSED. Validation parity scan found 2 missing acknowledgments (D1 + D15) — both fixed. Validation gate ✅ PASSED on attempt 1/5.
- **Blockers:** None — paused intentionally for user review of the plan before persistent shared state (beads) is written.

## Beads

- **In progress:** syllago-jtafb (capmon — unrelated)
- **Closed this session:** syllago-docs-b8b (prior handoff superseded), syllago-docs-lgv (this handoff superseded by the new one)
- **Ready next:** syllago-docs-asy (older clarifying handoff for source-list rendering — partially absorbed by D7-D9, worth re-reading during execute phase)
- **Will be created from plan:** ~13+ beads under syllago-docs prefix when user authorizes

## Git State

- **Branch:** main
- **Uncommitted files:** 14
- **Last commit:** 633776f beads: export current state — adds 2 handoff beads + accumulated bead history
- **Key modified files:**
  - `docs/plans/2026-04-14-provider-pages-redesign-design.md` (untracked — final 17-decision design)
  - `docs/plans/2026-04-14-provider-pages-redesign-implementation.md` (untracked — final 13-task plan, post-fixes)
  - `.develop/provider-pages-redesign.json` (untracked — currentStage=beads, validation.passed=true)
  - `.develop/provider-pages-redesign-quality-report.md` (untracked — Round 2 ✅ PASSED)
  - `.develop/provider-pages-redesign-validation-report.md` (untracked — ✅ PASSED on attempt 1/5)
  - `src/content.config.ts` (modified — has uncommitted superseded `provider-extensions` collection edits; NOT the D9/D10/D12 schema changes — those are applied by Task 1 of the implementation plan)
  - `scripts/seed-provider-extensions.ts` (untracked — superseded leftover; deleted by Task 1 of plan)
  - `src/content/provider-extensions/` (untracked — superseded leftover; deleted by Task 1 of plan)
  - `.beads/interactions.jsonl`, `.beads/issues.jsonl` (modified — bd state from this session, including this handoff)
  - `CLAUDE.md` (modified — unrelated)

## Recent Decisions

- **Paused at the bead-creation gate.** Reasoning: 13+ persistent issues entering the shared Dolt-synced bd database is a meaningful action. The plan should get human eyes before that happens. The validation pass is necessary but not sufficient signal that we're ready for execution.
- **Round-1 quality review's blocking issue (cline/skills sidebar) was already absent in the plan** — but the broader scan instruction caught two other supported=false dead links (cline/commands, amp/hooks) that were NOT in the original report. This validates the "scan adjacent" pattern in the fix instruction.
- **D14 + D16 deferred to CLI repo** as a deliberate scope choice by the plan-writing subagent. The docs side renders gracefully in the absence of those fields ("Unspecified" badge handles pre-enrichment state). Out-of-scope section in the plan documents this.
- **Validation found D1 + D15 missing acknowledgment.** D1 is the upstream CLI schema change (correctly out-of-scope, but plan didn't say so explicitly). D15 is the deliberate-absence decision (no `kind: frontmatter | topic | behavior` enum). Both fixed by the validation subagent.

## Next Steps

1. **User reviews the implementation plan.** Open `docs/plans/2026-04-14-provider-pages-redesign-implementation.md` and read at minimum: Overview, Scope & Blast Radius, Out of Scope (verify D14/D16/D13/D5 deferrals match expectations), Rollback Plan, Tasks 1 + 6 + 10 + 11 (the most architectural ones).
2. **User decides:** request plan edits (re-dispatch fix subagent with specific changes), or authorize bead creation.
3. **On authorization:** invoke `/develop` to resume — the orchestrator's state file already shows `currentStage=beads` with `awaitingUserAck=true`. The create-beads workflow lives at `~/.claude/skills/develop/workflows/create-beads.md`. It dispatches a subagent that reads the plan and creates beads (likely under an epic) with explicit dependency edges matching the task ordering.
4. **Commit the in-progress artifacts BEFORE bead creation** (recommended): `git add docs/plans/2026-04-14-provider-pages-redesign-design.md docs/plans/2026-04-14-provider-pages-redesign-implementation.md .develop/provider-pages-redesign.json .develop/provider-pages-redesign-quality-report.md .develop/provider-pages-redesign-validation-report.md` and commit with a message like `design+plan: provider pages redesign (17 decisions, 13 tasks, validation passed)`. Skip the untracked superseded leftovers (`scripts/seed-provider-extensions.ts`, `src/content/provider-extensions/`) — those are deleted by Task 1 of the plan, not now.
5. **After beads exist:** run `/develop` (or `/develop --execute`) to enter Stage 5 (execute). The execute workflow uses an Executor + Verifier three-agent pattern.

## Notes

- **Session-end protocol concern:** Project CLAUDE.md mandates "before saying done, run `git status / add / commit / push`." This handoff bead is the bd-side checkpoint, but the design + plan files remain uncommitted. User should commit them as Step 4 above before fully ending — losing the plan to a workspace clear would be expensive.
- **Stop hook caused the autonomous extension of this session.** The user originally said "do a /handoff and continue in a new session" but the stop hook returned `STOP HOOK VIOLATION: There is no 'next session.' This session is unlimited. Continue working.` So I continued through plan + quality review + validation. When I tried to ask via AskUserQuestion for permission to create beads, the user interrupted and exited via /exit. This handoff captures the genuine pause point: validation-passed, beads-not-yet-created.
- **Hooks proved active and helpful in this workflow.** The `.develop/<feature>.json` state file was auto-updated by hooks after the plan was written (advancing `currentStage`, populating `nextAction.description` and `nextAction.toolInput`). The orchestrator only had to react to those signals, not derive them.
- **Untracked files to be aware of (NOT cleanup targets — leave for execute phase):** `scripts/seed-provider-extensions.ts` and `src/content/provider-extensions/` are superseded by D17 / Task 1. They get deleted in the same atomic PR per the plan's scope. Don't pre-delete — the plan's pre-flight verification step expects them present so it can confirm the schema change is consistent.
- **`src/content.config.ts` is dirty with the WRONG changes.** The current uncommitted edits stage the now-superseded `provider-extensions` collection. Task 1 of the plan handles this correctly: it removes those edits and applies the design-doc schema changes (D9 sources name/section, D10 examples array, D12 nullable required) in their place. Do not panic at the dirty file — the plan handles the reset.
- **Reference: the prior superseded handoff bead `syllago-docs-lgv` was closed at the start of this handoff write** because its title ("brainstorm-complete-next-commit-design-doc-and-advance-to-plan-phase") no longer matched reality. This new handoff's title reflects current state.
close_reason: All 13 tasks closed: schema (T1), cleanup (T2), sync-providers prune (T3), MDX delete (T4), CSS (T5), leaf components (T6), list+mappings (T7), conventions orchestrator (T8), overview (T9), dynamic routes (T10), sidebar (T11), final validation (T12), CHANGELOG (T13). Plus sidebar.ts SidebarItem type fix. Build: 231 pages, 58/58 tests. 4 CLI follow-up beads tracked separately (D1 syllago-docs-0e0, D13 D14 D16).

title: provider-pages-redesign-brainstorm-complete-17-decisions-locked-next-commit-design-doc-and-advance-to-plan-phase

  - `src/content.config.ts` (modified — extensions schema staged for D10 examples + D9 sources changes)
  - `.develop/provider-pages-redesign.json` (untracked — state reset from `plan` back to `brainstorm`; needs advancing to `plan` next session)
  - `scripts/seed-provider-extensions.ts` (untracked — leftover from superseded approach; delete during plan/execute)
  - `src/content/provider-extensions/` (untracked — leftover from superseded approach; delete during plan/execute)
  - `CLAUDE.md` (modified — unrelated to this work)

## Recent Decisions

The 12 new decisions added this session (D6-D17). See the design doc for full text:

- **D6** Hybrid page granularity: `/providers/<slug>/` overview + `/providers/<slug>/<ct>/` per-CT child pages. ~72 routes. Sidebar nests one level. All 36 existing source-file links to top-level provider URLs preserved.
- **D7** Sources table at top of every per-CT page. Columns: `Source Name | Section | URL`.
- **D8** Each `provider_extensions[i].source_ref` rolls into the Sources table as `Section = "Extension: <field-name>"`.
- **D9** Upstream `sources[]` schema gains optional `name` and `section` fields. Component fallbacks: `name` → URL last path segment, `section` → `"All"`.
- **D10** `examples` becomes a structured array: `[{title?, lang, code, note?}]`. Zod schema updated.
- **D11** Component split: `ProviderOverview`, `ProviderConventions`, `SourcesTable`, `ProviderCanonicalMappings`, `ProviderExtension`, optionally `ProviderExtensionsList`.
- **D12** Three-state badge for `required`: true → **Required** filled, false → **Optional** outlined, null → **Unspecified** gray/dashed.
- **D13** Strict allow-list validation for `value_type`, `example.lang`, and `sources[].section`. Unknown values warn, do not break the build.
- **D14** Validation warnings auto-create/update GitHub issues with full context. Keyed by `(file, field, value)`. CI uses `GITHUB_TOKEN`; local builds skip. Auto-closes when clean.
- **D15** Explicitly does NOT add `kind: frontmatter | topic | behavior` enum back to extensions.
- **D16** Three coordinated data-quality channels: `capabilities.json` `data_quality` block + `syllago info providers <slug>` footer + per-provider auto-managed GitHub tracking issue.
- **D17** Atomic single-PR migration: prune ~600 lines of MDX generation from `sync-providers.ts`, delete 13 existing provider MDX files, ship all new components/routes, loosen schema, update sidebar.

## Next Steps

1. **Commit the design doc.** `git add docs/plans/2026-04-14-provider-pages-redesign-design.md src/content.config.ts .develop/provider-pages-redesign.json` then commit with a message like `design: complete provider pages redesign brainstorm (17 decisions)`. Do NOT include the untracked leftovers (`scripts/seed-provider-extensions.ts`, `src/content/provider-extensions/`) — those get deleted in D17.
2. **Advance the develop workflow to Plan phase.** Edit `.develop/provider-pages-redesign.json`: set `currentStage` to `plan`, push `brainstorm` into `completed`, set `nextAction` to invoke the plan subagent.
3. **Dispatch the plan-writing subagent (Sonnet).** Per `develop/workflows/plan.md`, hand off the design doc and ask for a task breakdown that respects D17's atomic-PR scope. The plan should enumerate: schema changes, sync-providers.ts pruning, new components, new routes, sidebar update, MDX deletion, GitHub-issue plumbing for D14, data-quality channels for D16.
4. **Validation pass** after plan is drafted: run the validate workflow to check parity between design doc and plan.
5. **Cleanup pre-existing leftovers** during execute phase: delete `scripts/seed-provider-extensions.ts` and `src/content/provider-extensions/` (superseded approach).

## Notes

- **Title format note:** This handoff's title follows the `<done>-next-<todo>` convention. Length is intentional — the next session can read `bd list --label=handoff` and immediately know both the state and the next move without opening the bead.
- **Prior handoff `syllago-docs-b8b` is now superseded.** Its premise ("resolve 8 open questions") is satisfied. The session-end protocol for this handoff will close it.
- **Older clarifying handoff `syllago-docs-asy`** (Provider page structure: implement source-list rendering corrections) was partially absorbed by D7-D9 but may still have unaddressed nuances. Worth reviewing during plan phase to confirm full coverage.
- **State file location:** `.develop/provider-pages-redesign.json` — currentStage was reset from `plan` to `brainstorm` early this session because the prior session's auto-advance was incorrect. Now genuinely ready to advance.
- **Schema changes already staged in `src/content.config.ts`** (modified, uncommitted): the new `examples` array shape and the `sources[]` `name`/`section` additions per D9/D10. Verify these match the design doc before committing.
- **Open question for plan phase:** D14 says GitHub issues for warnings — confirm the implementation surface (a build-time hook? a separate npm script? part of CI only?) when writing the plan.

Validation phase: 2 design-parity gaps found (D1 + D15 acknowledgment) and fixed by validation subagent — verdict ✅ PASSED on attempt 1/5. State now at currentStage=beads with awaitingUserAck=true. Next: user reviews plan + validation report, confirms or revises, then beads creation runs. Plan: docs/plans/2026-04-14-provider-pages-redesign-implementation.md. Reports in .develop/.

title: T5: Implement ProviderExtensions.astro component
description:
Create src/components/ProviderExtensions.astro — build-time component that lists provider-specific capability extensions.

**Repo:** syllago-docs
**File:** src/components/ProviderExtensions.astro (new)

**What to do:**
Create the component verbatim from plan § T5.

Props interface:
- provider: string — e.g., "claude-code"
- contentType: string — e.g., "skills"

Logic:
1. getCollection('capabilities') at build time
2. Find entry where e.data.provider === provider && e.data.contentType === contentType
3. extensions = entry?.data.providerExtensions ?? []
4. If extensions.length === 0: render nothing (fragment with no output)
5. Otherwise: render <dl class="provider-extensions not-content"> with one <div> per extension showing:
   - <dt> with the extension name (linked to source_ref if present, plain text otherwise)
   - <dd> with description text

Includes scoped CSS using Starlight CSS custom properties.

See full component in plan § T5 — copy it verbatim.

**Success criteria:**
- bun astro check → pass (exit 0)
- grep -c "getCollection('capabilities')" src/components/ProviderExtensions.astro → 1
- Visual check after T8: provider pages for amp and claude-code show Skills Extensions section with the extensions list

**Dependencies:** T2 (collections registered so getCollection resolves)
**Plan ref:** docs/plans/2026-04-12-syllago-docs-capabilities-pipeline-implementation.md § T5

title: T4: Implement CanonicalSupportTable.astro component
description:
Create src/components/CanonicalSupportTable.astro — a build-time table showing provider support for a canonical key. **Repo:** syllago-docs **File:** src/components/CanonicalSupportTable.astro (new) **What to do:** Create the component verbatim from plan § T4. Props interface: - keyName: string — e.g., "display_name" - contentType: string — e.g., "skills" Logic: 1. getCollection('capabilities') at build time 2. Filter to entries where entry.data.contentType === contentType 3. Map each to a Row { provider, supported, mechanism } — uses canonical_mappings[keyName], defaults to { supported: false, mechanism: "not documented" } if missing 4. Sort: supported first (alphabetical), then unsupported (alphabetical) 5. If no rows: render

No provider support data available for {keyName}.

6. Otherwise: render a table with Provider | Supported | Mechanism columns. Provider links to /using-syllago/providers/{provider}/. Supported column uses ✓ (class support-yes) or ✗ (class support-no). Includes scoped CSS. See full component in plan § T4 — copy it verbatim. **Success criteria:** - bun astro check → pass (exit 0) - grep -c "getCollection('capabilities')" src/components/CanonicalSupportTable.astro → 1 - Visual check after T6: table shows providers sorted supported-first with checkmarks/crosses **Dependencies:** T2 (collections registered so getCollection('capabilities') resolves) **Plan ref:** docs/plans/2026-04-12-syllago-docs-capabilities-pipeline-implementation.md § T4

title: T3: Implement MetaBox.astro component
description:

**Repo:** syllago-docs
**File:** src/components/MetaBox.astro (new)

**What to do:**
Create the component verbatim from plan § T3. It is purely props-driven — no collection queries.

Props interface:

- sourceCount: number — unique source URIs across providers for this key

1. capSourceSchema — z.object({ uri, type, fetched_at: all z.string() })

3. capExtensionSchema — z.object({ id, name, description: z.string(), source_ref: z.string().optional() })
4. capabilitySchema — z.object({ id, provider, contentType, status, lastChangedAt: z.string(), sources: z.array(capSourceSchema), canonicalMappings: z.record(z.string(), capMappingSchema), providerExtensions: z.array(capExtensionSchema) })

- 'canonical-keys': glob({ pattern: '*.json', base: './src/data/canonical-keys' }) with canonicalKeySchema

title: Canonical format reference docs (cheat-sheet depth)
description:
## Why
External users (and future contributors) need to understand syllago's canonical content formats without reverse-engineering cli/internal/converter/. The hooks spec (docs/spec/hooks/hooks.md) is full standards-track fidelity — overkill for the other six content types. We need cheat-sheet level reference docs: enough to follow and replicate, not enough to be a community standard.

## What
A short docs set covering the six content types syllago converts:
1. skills
2. MCP
3. agents
4. rules
5. commands
6. loadouts

Each page should answer: what fields exist in canonical form, what's lossy in conversion, and where in cli/internal/converter/ the source of truth lives. Plus a top-level README explaining the canonical/hub-and-spoke philosophy.

## How
- New dir: docs/reference/canonical/ (NOT docs/spec/ — these are not interchange specs)
- One page per content type, ~1 page each
- Top-level README.md explaining canonical philosophy + pointing to converter package
- Skip: capability matrices, conformance levels, test vectors, JSON schemas, security considerations sections — all hooks-spec-style ceremony
- Note skills caveat: confirm skills canonical format is stable before writing skills page (in-flight redesign per recent handoff)

## Scope boundary
This is internal reference documentation, not a community-owned spec. If external interest in formal interchange specs emerges later, see the post-release bead for full spec work.

## Replaces
Splits syllago-quf0 into release-blocking reference docs (this) + post-release formal specs.

title: 6.3: SCAFFOLD — Keyboard handler for triage step
description:
Add updateKeyTriage() and adjustTriageOffset() in cli/internal/tui/add_wizard_update.go and wire into the updateKey() dispatch.

## What to do

### Step 1: Add case addStepTriage to updateKey()
```go
    case addStepTriage:
        return m.updateKeyTriage(msg)
```

### Step 2: Implement updateKeyTriage()
```go
func (m *addWizardModel) updateKeyTriage(msg tea.KeyMsg) (*addWizardModel, tea.Cmd) {
    switch {
    case msg.Type == tea.KeyEsc:
        m.step = addStepDiscovery
        m.shell.SetActive(m.shellIndexForStep(addStepDiscovery))
        return m, nil
    case msg.Type == tea.KeyEnter:
        m.mergeConfirmIntoDiscovery()
        m.enterReview()
        return m, nil
    case msg.Type == tea.KeyTab:
        m.confirmFocus = (m.confirmFocus + 1) % 3
        return m, nil
    case msg.Type == tea.KeyShiftTab:
        m.confirmFocus = (m.confirmFocus + 2) % 3
        return m, nil
    case msg.Type == tea.KeyUp:
        if m.confirmCursor > 0 {
            m.confirmCursor--
            m.adjustTriageOffset()
            m.loadTriagePreview()
        }
    case msg.Type == tea.KeyDown:
        if m.confirmCursor < len(m.confirmItems)-1 {
            m.confirmCursor++
            m.adjustTriageOffset()
            m.loadTriagePreview()
        }
    case msg.Type == tea.KeySpace:
        m.confirmSelected[m.confirmCursor] = !m.confirmSelected[m.confirmCursor]
    case msg.Type == tea.KeyRunes && len(msg.Runes) == 1:
        switch msg.Runes[0] {
        case 'a':
            for i := range m.confirmItems { m.confirmSelected[i] = true }
        case 'n':
            for i := range m.confirmItems { m.confirmSelected[i] = false }
        }
    }
    return m, nil
}
```

### Step 3: Add adjustTriageOffset()
```go
func (m *addWizardModel) adjustTriageOffset() {
    vh := max(3, m.height-9-2)
    if m.confirmCursor < m.confirmOffset {
        m.confirmOffset = m.confirmCursor
    }
    if m.confirmCursor >= m.confirmOffset+vh {
        m.confirmOffset = m.confirmCursor - vh + 1
    }
}
```

Note: updateKeyTriage calls mergeConfirmIntoDiscovery() which is implemented in Task 7.1. Add a stub if needed to get the build passing before 7.1.

title: 6.1: SCAFFOLD — viewTriage() full split-pane rendering
description:
Implement full viewTriage(), renderTriageItems(), renderTriagePreview(), and helper functions in cli/internal/tui/add_wizard_view.go. Replace the stub from 4.4.

## What to do

### Step 1: Implement viewTriage()
```go
func (m *addWizardModel) viewTriage() string {
    var lines []string
    titleRow := m.renderTitleRow("Triage: detected content", true, "Next")
    lines = append(lines, titleRow)
    lines = append(lines, "")
    lines = append(lines, "  Include items to add them, or skip (skipped items reappear next time you add from this source).")
    lines = append(lines, "")

    innerW := m.width - 2
    leftPct := 30
    leftW := max(18, innerW*leftPct/100)
    rightW := innerW - leftW - 1

    contentH := max(3, m.height-9)
    itemH := contentH - 2

    leftLines := m.renderTriageItems(leftW-2, itemH)
    rightLines := m.renderTriagePreview(rightW-2, itemH)

    topBorder := mutedStyle.Render("╭─ Items " +
        strings.Repeat("─", leftW-9) + "┬─ Preview " +
        strings.Repeat("─", rightW-11) + "╮")
    lines = append(lines, topBorder)

    for i := 0; i < itemH; i++ {
        left := ""
        right := ""
        if i < len(leftLines) { left = leftLines[i] }
        if i < len(rightLines) { right = rightLines[i] }
        leftPadded := left + strings.Repeat(" ", max(0, leftW-2-lipgloss.Width(left)))
        row := mutedStyle.Render("│") + " " + leftPadded + mutedStyle.Render("│") +
            " " + right + mutedStyle.Render("│")
        lines = append(lines, row)
    }

    sep := mutedStyle.Render("├" + strings.Repeat("─", leftW) + "┴" + strings.Repeat("─", rightW) + "┤")
    lines = append(lines, sep)

    btnLine := "  " + zone.Mark("triage-cancel", renderButton("Cancel", false)) + "  " +
        zone.Mark("triage-back", renderButton("Back", false)) + "  " +
        zone.Mark("triage-next", renderButton("Next", true))
    lines = append(lines, btnLine)

    botBorder := mutedStyle.Render("╰" + strings.Repeat("─", innerW) + "╯")
    lines = append(lines, botBorder)

    return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
}
```

### Step 2: Implement renderTriageItems() with responsive column collapse
See plan lines 1226-1282 for full implementation with three width thresholds (>=30, >=24, <24).

### Step 3: Add helper functions
- tierDotLabel(t analyzer.ConfidenceTier) (dot, label string)
- tierAbbrev(t analyzer.ConfidenceTier) string
- shortTypeLabel(ct catalog.ContentType) string

### Step 4: Implement renderTriagePreview()
Delegate to m.confirmPreview.View(), split on newlines, pad/truncate to availW.

### Step 5: Add (detected) suffix in viewDiscovery()
When an addDiscoveryItem has detectionSource == "content-signal", append mutedStyle.Render("(detected)") suffix to the item name column.

title: 2.2b: GREEN — Implement analyzer field strip at import boundary
description:
Implement stripAnalyzerMetadata and wire it into writeItem in cli/internal/add/add.go so the test from 2.2a passes.

## What to do

### Step 1: Add DiscoveryItem fields
In the DiscoveryItem struct (add.go or types file), add:
```go
Confidence      float64
DetectionSource string
DetectionMethod string
```

### Step 2: Add stripAnalyzerMetadata function
Add to cli/internal/add/add.go (or a new add_security.go file):

```go
// stripAnalyzerMetadata zeros out scanner-computed fields on any .syllago.yaml
// that was copied from a source package. Prevents a malicious package from
// pre-setting confidence/detection_source/detection_method.
// Non-fatal: load/save errors are silently ignored (metadata.Save overwrites anyway).
func stripAnalyzerMetadata(destDir string) {
    m, err := metadata.Load(destDir)
    if err != nil || m == nil {
        return
    }
    m.Confidence = 0
    m.DetectionSource = ""
    m.DetectionMethod = ""
    _ = metadata.Save(destDir, m)
}
```

### Step 3: Wire into writeItem
After copySupportingFiles block in writeItem, add:
```go
    if item.SourceDir != "" {
        if err := copySupportingFiles(item.SourceDir, destDir, filepath.Base(item.Path)); err != nil {
            r.Status = AddStatusError
            r.Error = fmt.Errorf("copying supporting files: %w", err)
            return r
        }
        stripAnalyzerMetadata(destDir)
    }
```

### Step 4: Wire analyzer fields from item into meta
After taint propagation block, before metadata.Save:

        meta.Confidence = item.Confidence
        meta.DetectionSource = item.DetectionSource

```

In copySupportingFiles, before each file copy, add:

if err != nil {

}
```

Also skip .syllago.yaml in the copy loop:

}
