title: Redesign 'syllago convert' for provider-to-provider conversion
description:
Redesign the convert command to do true provider-to-provider conversion rather than library-to-provider rendering (which is now the 'show' command's job).

New signature: syllago convert <type> <name> --from <provider> --to <provider>

The command takes content from one provider's format and outputs it in another provider's format, going through canonical as the intermediate step (hub-and-spoke). If the source provider can be inferred (e.g., from the content's metadata), the --from flag could be optional.

This pairs with the new 'show' command (syllago-mjmy):
- show = view library content in any format (read-only, stdout)
- convert = transform between provider formats (the actual conversion tool)
- install = convert + write to provider config dir
close_reason: Implemented in 1e80a57: file mode + library mode, --from/--to/--type flags, 12 tests

title: Enhance 'syllago inspect' to show content and provider previews
description:
Enhance the existing inspect command instead of creating a new 'show' command.

Current behavior: shows metadata (name, type, source, path, description) and file listing.

New behavior:
1. Default: show metadata AND content (the actual file text, not just the filename)
2. --as <provider>: show the content converted to that provider's native format with a header labeling the format
3. Keep existing metadata display
4. For multi-file content (skills), show the primary file by default

Examples:
  syllago inspect coding-standards              # metadata + content
  syllago inspect coding-standards --as cursor  # content in Cursor .mdc format
  syllago inspect coding-standards --as codex   # content in Codex format

This replaces the need for a separate 'show' command and makes inspect the one-stop command for viewing content in any format.

Needs tests for: default content display, --as flag with multiple providers, --json output, multi-file skills, error cases (unknown item, unknown provider).
close_reason: Enhanced inspect command: default mode now shows primary file content, --as <provider> flag renders content in provider format with header. 4 new tests added (default content, --as text, --as JSON, unknown provider error). All 18 inspect tests pass.

title: Gate unimplemented CLI commands (export, sign, verify)
description:
Three CLI commands return 'not yet implemented' errors:
- export_cmd.go: runExport()
- sign_cmd.go: runSign(), runVerify()

Users discovering these commands will get confusing errors. Either:
A) Hide them entirely (don't register with cobra) until implemented
B) Gate behind an --experimental flag
C) Show a clear 'coming soon' message with context

close_reason: Closed

title: Add approval flow for project-local config modifications in sandbox
description:
Project-local config directories (.claude/, .gemini/, .codex/) are mounted RW in the sandbox without any approval flow. Changes are applied directly to the real project, unlike global config paths which go through staging/diff/approval.

A sandboxed provider can silently modify project configuration (e.g. .claude/rules/) without the user seeing or approving the changes.

Options:
A) Include project-local config dirs in the staging/diff mechanism (more secure, more complex)
B) Document the limitation explicitly and add a post-sandbox summary of project config changes (simpler, still informative)

Files: cli/internal/sandbox/profile.go, cli/internal/sandbox/bwrap.go, cli/internal/sandbox/runner.go

title: Close TOCTOU race in installer copyFile symlink check
description:
installer/copy.go:28-34 checks if destination is a symlink via os.Lstat(), then creates via os.Create(). Race window between check and create allows an attacker to replace the file with a symlink (e.g. to ~/.ssh/authorized_keys) between the two operations.

Fix: Use atomic replace pattern — write to temp file in same directory, then os.Rename() (which is atomic on POSIX). This eliminates the TOCTOU window entirely.

Files: cli/internal/installer/copy.go
close_reason: Closed

title: Harden sandbox env var injection surface
description:
Provider profiles inject environment variables via SandboxEnvVars (runner.go:173-177) without key-name validation. While env filtering (envfilter.go) uses an allowlist for HOST env vars, profile-injected vars bypass this.

A corrupted or future profile could inject dangerous env vars like LD_PRELOAD, PYTHONPATH, NODE_PATH that load malicious modules at runtime inside the sandbox.

Fix: Add a denylist of dangerous env var names (LD_PRELOAD, LD_LIBRARY_PATH, PYTHONPATH, NODE_PATH, PERL5LIB, etc.) and validate profile SandboxEnvVars keys against it before injection.

Files: cli/internal/sandbox/runner.go, cli/internal/sandbox/envfilter.go
close_reason: Closed

title: add hooks only reads global settings.json, ignores project-level
description:
syllago add hooks --from claude-code only looks at the global ~/.claude/settings.json. If the global file doesn't exist or has no hooks, it reports 'No settings.json found' even when the project-level .claude/settings.json has hooks.

The discovery code (syllago add --from claude-code with no args) correctly finds project-level hooks. But the add-hooks code path (runAddHooks) only processes the global settings file.

Related to syllago-f4z1 (global vs project collision). Both need the hook add pathway to read project-level settings.
close_reason: Already fixed in aa80bec (scope default changed from 'global' to 'all', E2E tests added). Bead was not referenced in that commit's Closes line.

title: Add scoping strategy for global vs project content in library
description:
When content is added from both global and project-level provider configs, the library should preserve the distinction. Options to evaluate:

1. Namespace by scope: 'hooks/global/before-tool-execute-shell' vs 'hooks/project/before-tool-execute-shell'
2. Prefer project-level and skip global when names collide (project is more specific)
3. Merge intelligently (combine hook arrays from both scopes into one item)
4. Add a --scope flag to syllago add: --scope=project, --scope=global, --scope=all

This is the design-level issue behind syllago-f4z1. Needs a decision before implementing.

title: Handle global vs project content name collisions in add
description:
When syllago add --from <provider> discovers content, it merges global and project-level sources. If both have content with the same derived name (e.g., a global hook and project hook both named 'before-tool-execute-shell' because they share the same event+matcher), the global version silently overwrites the project version.

Expected behavior: syllago should either (a) scope names by location (e.g., 'before-tool-execute-shell' vs 'before-tool-execute-shell-project'), (b) prefer project-level over global, or (c) prompt the user to choose. At minimum, warn when a collision occurs.

This affects hooks (derived names from event+matcher) and potentially rules/skills/agents if global and project dirs have same-named items.

Reproduction: Create a project with .claude/settings.json containing a PreToolUse/Bash hook. Also have ~/.claude/settings.json with a PreToolUse/Bash hook. Run syllago add hooks --from claude-code — only the global hook's commands are stored.
close_reason: Already fixed in aa80bec: collision handling via uniqueItemDir + SourceScope metadata check. E2E tests for both hooks (TestAddHooksE2E_BothScopes) and MCP (TestAddMcpE2E_NameCollision, TestAddMcpCollision) exist.

title: Implement hook execution audit logging
description:
Implement execution audit logging for hook invocations.

## Context
Jordan's enterprise security review P0 item #2. Without audit trails, enterprise teams cannot investigate incidents involving hooks. Every hook execution should be logged with enough detail for incident response.

## Why
Hooks execute arbitrary code with full user privileges. When something goes wrong (data exfiltration, file corruption, credential exposure), the first question is "which hooks ran during the breach timeframe?" Currently there's no answer.

## What to Implement (Plan Only)
1. Audit log format — timestamp, event, hook name, exit code, truncated command, duration
2. Log location — ~/.syllago/audit.log or configurable path
3. Log rotation — size-based or time-based rotation to prevent unbounded growth
4. Integration with hook execution in installer/hooks.go
5. CLI command to query audit logs — syllago audit --since 1h --event before_tool_execute

## Success Criteria
1. Plan specifies log format (structured JSON lines recommended)
2. Plan covers log rotation strategy
3. Plan includes integration points in existing codebase (which functions to modify)
4. Plan addresses performance impact (logging should not measurably slow hook execution)
5. Plan includes test cases for: log creation, rotation, query filtering
close_reason: Implementation plan written: docs/plans/implementation/audit-logging.md. All 5 success criteria PASS.

title: Implement cryptographic signing for hooks
description:
Implement cryptographic signing for hook manifests and scripts using Sigstore/cosign or GPG.

## Context
Jordan's enterprise security review (docs/reviews/spec-design-audit-enterprise-security.md) identified content hashing as insufficient for enterprise trust — it verifies integrity but not provenance. The spec defines a signatures field; this bead implements the signing workflow.

## Why
Without signing, any compromise of a registry git repo or MITM on clone silently replaces hook content. Content hashing only detects tampering if you have an independent trusted hash. Enterprise deployment (200+ devs) needs cryptographic provenance.

## What to Implement (Plan Only)
1. signatures field in .syllago.yaml — detached signature storage
2. syllago sign command — sign a hook directory's manifest + scripts
3. syllago verify command — verify signatures against a configurable trust policy
4. Trust policy configuration — keyring of trusted signing keys
5. Integration with install flow — verify before install, warn/block on unsigned

## Success Criteria
1. Plan specifies exact file paths, function signatures, and CLI command interfaces
2. Plan covers both Sigstore/cosign and GPG approaches with a recommendation
3. Plan addresses key management (where do keys live, how are they distributed)
4. Plan includes test cases for: signed+valid, signed+tampered, unsigned, expired signature
5. Plan considers the solo developer case (signing should be optional, not required)
close_reason: Implementation plan written: docs/plans/implementation/signing.md. All 5 success criteria PASS.

title: Implement syllago convert --batch for hook migration
description: Implement syllago convert --batch for automated hook migration from provider-native to canonical format. Sam's review: migration of 30+ hooks is 2-3 hours manually. This command should do it in minutes. Plan should cover: CLI interface (syllago convert --from claude-code --batch ./hooks/), per-hook conversion with warnings, output directory structure, handling of inline vs script-file hooks, dry-run mode. Success criteria: specifies CLI flags, covers all source providers, handles edge cases (malformed hooks, unsupported events), includes test cases.

title: Implement script content scanning for hooks
description: Implement script content scanning for hook directories. Current ScanHookSecurity only checks command strings in manifests, never opens the actual script files. The scripts are the attack surface, not the manifest. Plan should: extend scanner to scan all executable files in hook directory, scan string values in env/cwd/platform/provider_data fields, add file-type-specific patterns for Python/Node/PowerShell, report findings per-file with line numbers. Success criteria: specifies file extensions to scan, covers provider_data scanning, includes new patterns for non-shell languages, addresses performance, includes test cases for clean and malicious scripts. See Jordan review section 2.

title: Add 'syllago add --from shared' for bundled content
description:
WHY: Syllago bundles skills in content/skills/ (syllago-guide, syllago-import, syllago-provider-audit, etc.) but there's no workflow to get them into the user's library. syllago add requires --from <provider>, and shared content isn't a provider. Users who clone a repo with bundled content can see it in syllago list ([shared] tag) but can't install or convert it.

USE CASE: User clones syllago, runs make build, wants to use the built-in syllago-provider-audit skill. Today they're stuck — no command path from shared content to library to installed.

WHAT TO BUILD:
- syllago add --from shared: discovers content in the repo's content/ directory and adds to library
- syllago add <name> --from shared: adds a specific item
- syllago add --from shared --all: adds all bundled content
- The shared content dir is already scanned by catalog (shows as [shared] in list) — the gap is in the add command's provider requirement

AFFECTS: Any repo that bundles syllago content, not just the syllago repo itself. Teams could put skills in their project's content/ dir.

IMPLEMENTATION HINTS:
- cli/cmd/syllago/add_cmd.go: the --from flag validates against provider.AllProviders — need to also accept "shared"
- When --from shared, scan the project's content/ dir instead of a provider's config dir
- The rest of the add flow (copy to library, metadata creation) should work as-is
close_reason: Implemented in 62da6d4

title: Bug: syllago add only copies primary content file, drops supporting files
description:
When adding a skill (or other content type) that has supporting files in subdirectories, syllago add only copies the primary file (e.g., SKILL.md) and ignores everything else (workflows/, scripts/, references/, etc.).

This means:
1. Library copy is incomplete — skill won't work correctly without its reference files
2. syllago publish also only publishes the single file (since library is the source)
3. Users silently lose content with no warning

Reproduction:
- Have a skill with subdirectory files: skills/atlassian/SKILL.md + skills/atlassian/workflows/**
- Run: syllago add skills/atlassian --from claude-code
- Check library: only SKILL.md is present, workflows/ directory is missing

Expected: All files in the skill directory tree should be copied to the library.

Root cause: The add workflow (internal/add package) likely uses single-file copy logic rather than directory-recursive copy. Need to trace AddItems() and DiscoverFromProvider() to find where files are enumerated.

Impact: Any multi-file skill or content item is silently broken after add. This affects the entire add → library → publish pipeline.
close_reason: Fixed: added SourceDir field to DiscoveryItem, copySupportingFiles() in writeItem(), 4 new tests

title: TUI: git fetch on Init() blocks terminal input when credentials unavailable
description:
## Problem

The TUI's `Init()` method (app.go:178) calls `checkForUpdate()` which runs `git fetch origin main --quiet --tags` as an async Bubble Tea Cmd. When git credentials are not available (private repo, expired token, VHS recording, CI), git prompts for `Username for 'https://github.com':` on the terminal.

This prompt **steals stdin from the TUI**, making all keyboard navigation impossible. The TUI renders but is completely frozen — no keystrokes reach Bubble Tea because the git subprocess owns the pty input.

## Root Cause

`update.go:609` — the `exec.Command("git", ...)` inherits the process's stdin/stdout/stderr. When git needs credentials, it prompts on the same terminal the TUI is using.

```go
// update.go:606-610
func checkForUpdate(repoRoot, localVersion string) tea.Cmd {
    return func() tea.Msg {
        fetchCmd := exec.Command("git", "-C", repoRoot, "fetch", "origin", "main", "--quiet", "--tags")
        fetchCmd.Run() // ← inherits stdin, blocks on credential prompt
```

## Impact

- TUI is completely unusable when credentials aren't cached
- Affects VHS/demo recordings (no credential helper available)
- Affects CI/CD environments
- Affects users who cloned via HTTPS without a credential helper
- No timeout — hangs indefinitely

## Recommended Fix

Three changes in `update.go`, `checkForUpdate()`:

### 1. Suppress credential prompts (one-line fix)
```go
fetchCmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), "GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0")
```
This makes git fail silently instead of prompting. The existing `fetchCmd.Run()` already ignores errors, so this is safe.

### 2. Detach from terminal
```go
fetchCmd.Stdin = nil
fetchCmd.Stdout = nil  
fetchCmd.Stderr = nil
```
Prevents git from ever touching the TUI's terminal.

### 3. Add a timeout (defense in depth)
```go
ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
defer cancel()
fetchCmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", "-C", repoRoot, "fetch", "origin", "main", "--quiet", "--tags")
```

## Additional Consideration

The `registryAutoSync` setting exists in settings.go but registry sync is user-triggered (press `s`). The `Init()` update check is NOT registry sync — it's a version check against the syllago repo itself. Consider:
- Throttling to once per day (cache last check timestamp)
- Making it a setting (like `registryAutoSync`) that users can disable
- Showing a non-blocking notification instead of blocking launch

## Files

- `cli/internal/tui/update.go` — lines 606-610 (`checkForUpdate`)
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — line 178 (`Init()`)

## How to Reproduce

```bash
# Record with VHS (no credential helper in pty)
HOME=/tmp/clean-home vhs demo.tape

# Or run with expired/missing credentials
GIT_ASKPASS=false syl
# TUI renders but keyboard input is frozen
```
close_reason: Fixed git fetch stdin steal + added Update check setting

title: Fix: install command ignores per-type path overrides from config
description:
## Problem

The `syllago install` command (`cli/cmd/syllago/install_cmd.go`) constructs a PathResolver at line 90 but only uses `resolver.BaseDir()` at line 140, passing a flat `baseDir` string to `installer.Install()`. This means per-type config path overrides (e.g., `provider_paths.claude-code.paths.skills = "/custom/skills"`) have NO effect on `syllago install`.

The resolver priority chain is: per-type path > CLI --base-dir > config baseDir > default. Currently `install` only honors the bottom three tiers. The `sync-and-export` and `loadout apply` commands both use the resolver correctly.

## Root Cause

`installer.Install()` (cli/internal/installer/installer.go:163) accepts `baseDir string` — a flat directory override. It always calls `prov.InstallDir(baseDir, ct)` internally. There's no way to pass "use this exact install directory" without changing the function signature.

The resolver's per-type path returns a final install directory directly (bypassing `prov.InstallDir()` entirely), but `Install()` has no mechanism to accept that.

## Fix Approach

Add `InstallWithResolver(item, prov, repoRoot, method, resolver)` to `cli/internal/installer/installer.go` that:
1. Checks `IsJSONMerge` first (unchanged — merge types don't use resolver)
2. Uses `resolver.InstallDir(prov, item.Type, home)` to get the effective install dir
3. Computes the target path from that resolved dir (same logic as `CheckStatusWithResolver`)
4. Proceeds with symlink/copy/converter logic using the resolved path

Update `install_cmd.go` to call `InstallWithResolver` instead of `Install` with `resolver.BaseDir()`.

Keep existing `Install()` unchanged for backward compatibility (loadout apply and other callers that pass baseDir directly).

## Key Files

- `cli/cmd/syllago/install_cmd.go` — line 140 is the call site
- `cli/internal/installer/installer.go` — `Install()` at line 163, `CheckStatusWithResolver()` at line 114 (reference for resolver usage pattern)
- `cli/internal/config/resolver.go` — `InstallDir()` method

## Success Criteria

1. `syllago config paths set claude-code --type skills --path /custom/skills` followed by `syllago install my-skill --to claude-code` installs the skill to `/custom/skills/my-skill`, not `~/.claude/skills/my-skill`
2. Existing `--base-dir` flag still works
3. Config-level `base_dir` still works
4. Per-type path > CLI --base-dir > config baseDir > default priority chain holds
5. JSON merge types (hooks, MCP) are unaffected
6. Unit test for `InstallWithResolver` covering per-type path override
7. L2 command test verifying `install` command respects config per-type paths
8. All existing tests pass (no regressions)
close_reason: Added InstallWithResolver() to installer.go, updated install_cmd.go to use it. 7 new unit tests + 1 L2 integration test. All 8 success criteria validated.

title: mouse: add click-to-select for Files/Preview tabs in split-view detail
description:
The Files and Preview tabs in the split-view detail panel cannot be selected by clicking. Mouse scrolling only works on the active tab, but there's no way to switch tabs with the mouse — only keyboard works.

Expected behavior: clicking the Files or Preview tab header should switch the active tab, and mouse scrolling should then work on whichever tab is active.

Note: scrolling content based on mouse cursor position (hover-to-activate) would be nice but may not be feasible with BubbleTea's zone system. The minimum fix is click-to-select on tab headers.

Files: cli/internal/tui/split_view.go, cli/internal/tui/detail.go, cli/internal/tui/detail_render.go

title: Task 7: Update session close protocol in beads context
description:
Add /handoff as step 3.5 in the session close protocol checklist so it's part of the standard close flow.

Files to modify: Locate the source file first:
- find ~/.config/pai ~/.local -name '*.md' | xargs grep -l 'SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL'
- find ~/.beads -name '*.md' | xargs grep -l 'SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL'

Updated checklist order:
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. bd sync                 (commit beads changes)
[ ] 3.5. /handoff              (write .handoff.json for next session)
[ ] 4. git commit -m "..."    (commit code)
[ ] 5. bd sync                 (commit any new beads changes)
[ ] 6. git push                (push to remote)

Note: /handoff runs bd sync internally (step 6 of the workflow), so step 5 may be a no-op after /handoff — that's expected belt-and-suspenders behavior.

Success criteria: bd prime 2>&1 | grep -A 12 'SESSION CLOSE' shows the updated checklist with step 3.5.

title: Task 3: Create session-handoff-read.ts hook
description:
Create the SessionStart hook that reads .handoff.json from the project root and injects it as a context block into Claude's session.

Files to create: ~/.config/pai/hooks/session-handoff-read.ts

Key design decisions:
- Resolves project root by walking up from cwd looking for .git/ or .handoff.json
- Outputs via console.log (stdout) — same pattern as load-core-context.ts
- Skips if file doesn't exist (first session), is malformed, or project root can't be determined
- Skips for subagent sessions (same guard as load-core-context.ts)
- Reads SessionStart payload from stdin (JSON with session_id, cwd fields)

Output format: <system-reminder> block with formatted handoff sections (ACTIVE WORK, BEADS, GIT STATE, RECENT DECISIONS, NEXT STEPS, NOTES, PREVIOUS SESSIONS)

Success criteria: echo '{"session_id":"test","cwd":"/home/hhewett/.local/src/syllago"}' | bun run ~/.config/pai/hooks/session-handoff-read.ts — exits cleanly with no output when no .handoff.json exists; outputs formatted context block when it does exist.

title: Task E2E: Provider smoke tests (Claude Code + Gemini CLI)
description:
# Provider Smoke Tests

## Why
File-level tests verify that loadout apply puts files in the right places. But only real CLI tools can confirm the content is actually picked up. These smoke tests catch provider path changes, config parsing quirks, and permission issues.

## Description
Separate CI workflow that installs Claude Code and Gemini CLI, applies loadouts, and uses programmatic mode (-p flag) to verify content is loaded.

### CI Workflow
- Runs on release branches and manual trigger only (not every PR)
- Gates releases
- Separate jobs per provider
- Auth: SSO/service account (setup TBD)

### Claude Code Tests
- Apply kitchen-sink loadout, verify rules active via claude -p
- Verify MCP servers via claude mcp list (deterministic, preferred)
- Verify commands available
- Clean removal verification
- Edge cases: first-time setup, merge with existing config, sequential loadouts

### Gemini CLI Tests
- Apply loadout, verify rules via gemini -p
- Verify MCP config in settings.json
- Clean removal

### Security
- Don't log full CLI output (prevent secret leakage)
- Pin test content to known-good commits
- assert_contains uses grep -qF (fixed-string, no eval)
- Prefer deterministic introspection commands over LLM-based assertions

## Success Criteria
- CI workflow exists at .github/workflows/smoke-test-providers.yml
- Claude Code smoke tests pass
- Gemini CLI smoke tests pass
- Edge case tests pass (first-time, merge, sequential)
- No secrets leaked in CI logs

## Test-Driven Development
1. Write test scripts first (tests/smoke/claude-code.sh, tests/smoke/gemini-cli.sh)
2. Implement assert_contains with grep -qF
3. Test locally before wiring into CI
4. Add CI workflow

## Key Files
- tests/smoke/claude-code.sh (NEW)
- tests/smoke/gemini-cli.sh (NEW)
- .github/workflows/smoke-test-providers.yml (NEW)

## Open Questions
- CI authentication: how to persist SSO/OAuth on runners
- "Try" mode: verify SessionEnd hook injection rather than actual revert
close_reason: Created E2E provider smoke test suite: tests/smoke/helpers.sh (shared framework with assert_contains using grep -qF, temp HOME isolation, assertion failure tracking), tests/smoke/claude-code.sh (7 tests: first-time apply, clean removal, merge with existing config, sequential loadouts, double-apply prevention, MCP introspection, preview mode), tests/smoke/gemini-cli.sh (4 tests: cross-provider apply, clean removal, merge with existing, sequential loadouts + 3 auth-gated). CI workflow at .github/workflows/smoke-test-providers.yml with separate jobs per provider. Discovered MCP merge bug (syllago-0sbe).

title: Task 2.2: Delete all README.md files from content/
description:
# Delete All README.md Files

## Why
With README infrastructure removed (Task 2.1), existing README.md files are dead weight. Clean break — delete them all.

## Description
Remove all 11 README.md files from content/. Before deleting, check if any contain useful information not present in the primary content file and migrate it first.

Files to delete:
- content/apps/example-kitchen-sink-app/README.md
- content/hooks/claude-code/example-lint-on-save/README.md
- content/loadouts/claude-code/example-kitchen-sink-loadout/README.md
- content/rules/claude-code/example-concise-output/README.md
- content/rules/cursor/example-concise-output/README.md
- content/commands/claude-code/example-review/README.md
- content/skills/syllago-import/README.md
- content/local/agents/d2-diagram-expert.md/README.md
- content/local/agents/research-agent.md/README.md
- content/local/agents/pr-verify.md/README.md
- content/local/skills/building-agentic-systems/README.md

## Success Criteria
- No README.md files exist under content/
- Useful info migrated to primary content files where needed
- make test passes
- Catalog scanner finds all content items

## Test-Driven Development
1. Verify each README.md content before deleting (check for unique info)
2. After deletion, run make test to confirm no breakage
3. Verify catalog scan still finds all items (integration test)

## Key Files
- All README.md files listed above

title: Post-add success message: poor placement and warning overflow
description:
## Bug
After adding items via provider discovery (e.g., "Add provider → Gemini → add selected"):

1. **Success message placement**: The "Added [item]" message with the full list of added items renders at the very top of the welcome/landing page. When many items are added, this creates a long block of text that pushes the actual content down. It's not obvious and looks bad.

2. **Warning overflow**: After the success message, a large number of warnings scatter across the screen. The warnings are not contained or organized — they just dump all over the visible area.

## Expected
- Success message should be in a scrollable modal or summary panel, not inline at the top of the landing page
- Warnings should be grouped and contained (e.g., collapsible section, or "N warnings" with expand option)
- For bulk adds, a summary ("Added 12 items to library") with optional detail expansion

## Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — statusMessage / statusWarnings rendering in renderContentWelcome()
- The status message system renders a flat string + flat warning list — doesn't scale to bulk operations

## Context
This is the same statusMessage system we just improved (statusIsErr, keypress clearing). The core rendering works for single-item operations but breaks down for bulk adds with many warnings.
close_reason: Closed

title: Registry card: 'v─' version display confusing when no manifest
description:
## Bug
When a registry has no manifest version, the card shows "v─" which is confusing. The "v" prefix combined with "─" (em dash placeholder) reads as a version string rather than "no version available".

## Files
- `cli/internal/tui/registries.go` — renderRegistryCard() version formatting (line ~109-112)

## Current Code
```go
version := "─"
if entry.version != "" {
    version = entry.version
}
meta := fmt.Sprintf("%s  v%s  %d items", status, helpStyle.Render(version), entry.itemCount)
```

## Options
- Show "v─" as just "─" (no "v" prefix when no version)
- Show nothing for version when absent
- Show "no version" text
close_reason: Closed

title: Clear status messages on keypress
description:
## Phase 3: Clear status messages on keypress

### Problem
statusMessage is write-only. Once set, it persists forever. Stale messages mislead users about current state.

### Implementation
Add at the TOP of the tea.KeyMsg case in App.Update(), BEFORE delegating to screen-specific handlers:

```go
case tea.KeyMsg:
    // Clear transient status on any keypress
    if a.statusMessage != "" {
        a.statusMessage = ""
        a.statusIsErr = false
        a.statusWarnings = nil
    }
```

This matches the existing pattern in settings.go (~line 55).

Only clear on actual keypresses (tea.KeyMsg), not on async message completions or other msg types.

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — tea.KeyMsg handler in App.Update()

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Status message, statusIsErr, and statusWarnings are cleared on any keypress
- [ ] Clearing happens BEFORE screen-specific key handling (so the keypress that clears also does its normal action)
- [ ] Async message completions (registryAddDoneMsg, etc.) do NOT trigger clearing
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not add timer-based clearing (keypress-based is sufficient per plan)
close_reason: Closed

title: Clone cleanup on all dismiss paths
description:
## Phase 2d: Clone cleanup on all dismiss paths

### Problem
If user dismisses the "Not a Syllago Registry" confirmation modal without confirming, the preserved clone must be cleaned up.

### Implementation
In the handler for the confirmation modal's dismiss/cancel:

1. **Cancel button clicked** -> os.RemoveAll(a.pendingNonSyllagoClone)
2. **Esc pressed** -> same cleanup (both handled in the modal's dismiss path)
3. **User confirms -> import flow** -> importModel takes ownership via initFromExternalClone (Phase 2c handles this)

Note: Sidebar clicks during a modal are NOT reachable (focus is trapped to focusModal), so that path doesn't need handling.

After cleanup on cancel/esc paths, clear the pending fields:
```go
os.RemoveAll(a.pendingNonSyllagoClone)
a.pendingNonSyllagoClone = ""
a.pendingNonSyllagoScan = catalog.NativeScanResult{}
```

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — confirmation modal cancel/dismiss handler

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Cancel removes the clone directory (os.RemoveAll)
- [ ] Esc removes the clone directory
- [ ] pendingNonSyllagoClone is cleared to "" after cleanup
- [ ] pendingNonSyllagoScan is cleared after cleanup
- [ ] Confirm path does NOT clean up (import model takes ownership)
- [ ] No leaked temp directories after any dismiss path
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not modify import.go (Phase 2c handles that)
close_reason: Closed

title: Bridge registry redirect to import flow
description:
## Phase 2c: Bridge registry redirect to import flow

### Problem
When user confirms "Browse and import?" modal, need to hand off the preserved clone to the import model and switch to the import screen.

### Implementation

**1. New method on importModel (import.go):**
```go
func (m *importModel) initFromExternalClone(path string) {
    m.clonedPath = path  // takes ownership for cleanup
    m.fromRegistryRedirect = true
    m.message = ""
    m.messageIsErr = false
    m.pickCursor = 0

    cat, err := catalog.Scan(path, path)
    if err != nil || len(cat.Items) == 0 {
        // Content detected by native scan but not importable via catalog.Scan
        m.step = stepGitURL
        m.message = "Detected provider content but no importable items. Use CLI: syllago add <path>"
        m.messageIsErr = true
        m.cleanup()
        return
    }
    m.clonedItems = cat.Items
    m.step = stepGitPick
}
```

**2. Add fromRegistryRedirect field to importModel (import.go):**
```go
fromRegistryRedirect bool
```

**3. Modify Esc handler in updateGitPick (import.go ~line 709-713):**
When m.fromRegistryRedirect is true, instead of going to stepGitURL:
- Call m.cleanup()
- Clear m.fromRegistryRedirect
- Return a message (e.g., importBackToRegistriesMsg{}) that tells App.Update to switch to screenRegistries

**4. Handle confirm in App.Update (app.go):**
When the "Browse" confirmation is accepted:
```go
a.importer.cleanup()  // clean up any previous clone
a.importer.initFromExternalClone(a.pendingNonSyllagoClone)
a.pendingNonSyllagoClone = ""  // import model owns it now
a.pendingNonSyllagoScan = catalog.NativeScanResult{}
a.screen = screenImport
```

**5. Handle importBackToRegistriesMsg in App.Update:**
```go
a.screen = screenRegistries
```

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/import.go` — initFromExternalClone method, fromRegistryRedirect field, updateGitPick Esc handler
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — confirm handler, importBackToRegistriesMsg handler

### Success Criteria
- [ ] initFromExternalClone method exists on importModel
- [ ] fromRegistryRedirect bool field exists on importModel
- [ ] Confirming "Browse" switches to screenImport with items populated
- [ ] Esc from stepGitPick when fromRegistryRedirect returns to screenRegistries (not stepGitURL)
- [ ] Clone cleanup happens via import model's cleanup() on Esc
- [ ] Scan strategy mismatch handled: if catalog.Scan finds nothing, shows explanatory message and cleans up
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not modify native_scan.go
- Do not add new import wizard steps — reuse existing stepGitPick
close_reason: Closed

title: Handle non-syllago message with confirmation modal
description:

### Implementation


2. Add handler in App.Update() for registryAddNonSyllagoMsg:

   - Show a confirmation modal:
     - Title: "Not a Syllago Registry"
     - Body: "This repository contains [provider list] content but isn't a syllago registry.\n\nBrowse and import individual items?"
     - Two buttons: "Browse" (confirm) and "Cancel"

3. The confirmation modal can use the existing modal patterns in the codebase (check how other confirmation modals work in app.go/modal.go)

- [ ] registryAddNonSyllagoMsg handler stores clone path and scan data
- [ ] Confirmation modal appears with detected provider descriptions
- [ ] registryOpInProgress is set to false

- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

- Do not implement the confirm action (bridge to import — that's Phase 2c)

title: Fix status rendering on welcome screen
description:
## Phase 1c: Fix status rendering on welcome screen

### Problem
app.go:2103-2108 always renders statusMessage with successMsgStyle and "Done:" prefix — even for errors. In-progress messages also get the "Done:" prefix.

### Implementation
Replace the existing status rendering block (~lines 2103-2108) with:
```go
if a.statusMessage != "" {
    if a.statusIsErr {
        s += errorMsgStyle.Render(a.statusMessage) + "\n"
    } else {
        s += successMsgStyle.Render(a.statusMessage) + "\n"
    }
    for _, w := range a.statusWarnings {
        s += warningStyle.Render("Warning: " + w) + "\n"
    }
    s += "\n"
}
```

Key changes:
- Use statusIsErr to select error vs success style
- Drop the "Done:" prefix entirely (past tense messages like "Added registry: foo" already communicate completion)
- Keep warning rendering for statusWarnings

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` around lines 2103-2108 (renderContentWelcome)

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Error messages render with errorMsgStyle (not green successMsgStyle)
- [ ] Success messages render with successMsgStyle
- [ ] No "Done:" prefix on any status message
- [ ] statusWarnings still render with warningStyle
- [ ] In-progress messages don't get "Done:" prefix
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes (golden files may need updating)

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not change how statusMessage is set — only how it's rendered
close_reason: Closed

title: Extract rebuildRegistryState helper
description:
## Phase 1d: Extract rebuildRegistryState helper

### Problem
Three done-message handlers (registryAddDoneMsg ~line 601, registryRemoveDoneMsg ~line 623, registrySyncDoneMsg ~line 648) duplicate ~10 lines of config reload + model rebuild + catalog rescan.

### Implementation
Extract a shared helper method:

```go
func (a *App) rebuildRegistryState() {
    cfg, err := config.Load(a.catalog.RepoRoot)
    if err == nil {
        a.registryCfg = cfg
    }
    a.registries = newRegistriesModel(a.catalog.RepoRoot, a.registryCfg, a.catalog)
    a.registries.width = a.width - sidebarWidth - 1
    a.registries.height = a.panelHeight()
    a.sidebar.registryCount = len(a.registryCfg.Registries)
    cat, scanErr := catalog.ScanWithGlobalAndRegistries(a.catalog.RepoRoot, a.projectRoot, a.registrySources)
    if scanErr == nil {
        a.catalog = cat
        a.refreshSidebarCounts()
    }
}
```

Replace the duplicated blocks in all three handlers with `a.rebuildRegistryState()`.

**Cursor adjustment:** The registryRemoveDoneMsg handler also clamps `a.cardCursor` after rebuild (app.go:637-639). This stays OUTSIDE the helper — the remove handler calls `rebuildRegistryState()` then does the clamp. Add and sync handlers just call the helper directly.

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` lines 601-663 (three done-message handlers)

### Success Criteria
- [ ] New `rebuildRegistryState()` method exists on *App
- [ ] All three done-message handlers (add, remove, sync) call rebuildRegistryState()
- [ ] Remove handler still clamps a.cardCursor AFTER calling the helper
- [ ] No duplicated config reload / model rebuild / catalog rescan code remains in the handlers
- [ ] Each handler still sets its own statusMessage and registryOpInProgress before calling the helper
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not add statusIsErr logic yet (that's Phase 1a)
- Do not change what the handlers do with statusMessage — just extract the shared rebuild
close_reason: Closed

title: Fail-fast name validation before clone
description:
## Phase 0b: Fail-fast name validation before clone

### Problem
Name derivation (registry.NameFromURL) and duplicate/invalid checks happen inside the async command (app.go:218-228), after the modal closes and clone begins. These can be checked synchronously before the expensive network operation.

### Implementation
At app.go:1160-1171, in the handler that detects `!a.registryAddModal.active`:

1. After modal confirms (`a.registryAddModal.confirmed`), derive name from URL (or use override)
2. Check `catalog.IsValidRegistryName(name)` — if invalid, set modal error, re-open modal
3. Check for duplicates in `a.registryCfg.Registries` — if duplicate, set modal error, re-open modal
4. Only call `doRegistryAdd` if all validation passes

**Re-opening the modal on failure:**
```go
a.registryAddModal.message = fmt.Sprintf("Invalid registry name: %s", name)
a.registryAddModal.messageIsErr = true
a.registryAddModal.active = true
a.registryAddModal.confirmed = false
a.focus = focusModal  // keep input routing consistent
```
This works because modal's View() checks `if !m.active { return "" }`.

**Remove the duplicate checks from inside doRegistryAdd** (app.go:218-228) since they now happen synchronously. The async command can assume the name is valid and unique.

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` lines 1160-1171 (modal confirm handler) and 218-228 (remove redundant checks)

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Invalid name shows error in modal and keeps it open (user can edit and retry)
- [ ] Duplicate name shows error in modal and keeps it open
- [ ] a.focus is set to focusModal when re-activating the modal
- [ ] doRegistryAdd no longer does name validation (moved to synchronous path)
- [ ] Empty URL validation still works (already handled by modal.go)
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not change the modal.go Update method (it already sets confirmed=true, active=false on Enter)
- Do not touch doRegistryAdd's clone/scan logic — only remove the name validation from it
close_reason: Closed

title: 4.1: Refactor renderContent() to Put Metadata Above Separator
description:
Refactors the detail view rendering to split content into a pinned header (item name, tab bar, metadata) that always shows and a scrollable body (tab content only), so Type/Path/Provider metadata is always visible above the separator.

Files:
- Modify: cli/internal/tui/detail_render.go (lines 15-50 renderContent, renderOverviewTab)
- Modify: cli/internal/tui/detail.go (clampScroll method)

Steps:
1. Replace renderContent() with a thin wrapper that calls renderContentSplit()
2. Add renderContentSplit() returning (pinned string, body string): pinned contains name+position+LOCAL tag+tab bar+metadata block+separator; body contains tab content
3. Remove Type/Path/Provider metadata lines from renderOverviewTab() (now in pinned header)

title: Rename 'provider' terminology in Syllago
description:
Syllago uses 'provider' for AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, etc.). This term is inaccurate — 12/14 tools self-identify as 'agents' and the industry has converged on that term. 'Provider' also conflicts with 'model provider' (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google).

Previous decision (feedback_terminology.md): use 'agent' publicly, 'harness' internally, never 'provider' for agents.

Complication: MOAT uses 'subagent' for the content type. If the tools become 'agents' and the content type is also agent-adjacent, terminology needs careful design. The MOAT terminology resolution (2026-04-13) established that MOAT's naming is correct at the protocol level — tools are 'AI Agent Runtimes', content is 'subagents'. But Syllago's user-facing terminology is a separate question.

Scope: cli/internal/provider/ (23 files), Provider struct, CLI flags, TUI labels, installers, converters. Hundreds of references. This is a large refactor.

Options explored: 'agent' (collides with content type), 'runtime' (too technical), 'harness' (designated internal-only). Needs further brainstorming.

Decoupled from MOAT integration — boundary mapping handles the protocol boundary regardless of internal naming.

title: process-issues.md: add branch-first step before committing format doc changes
New content type: Checks (code review rules)
description:
## Summary

Add 'checks' as a new content type in syllago. Checks are structured code review rules with YAML frontmatter, first introduced by Amp. This is a significant finding — no other AI coding tool manager supports this content type yet, so having it at launch would be a differentiator.

## Background

Discovered during research into Amp's .agents/checks/ feature (2026-04-08). Research doc: docs/research/agents-directory-cross-provider-research.md

## Amp Checks Format (Primary Source: https://ampcode.com/manual)

File type: Markdown (.md) with YAML frontmatter
Paths:
  - .agents/checks/          — project-wide
  - subdir/.agents/checks/   — subtree-scoped
  - ~/.config/agents/checks/ — global (primary)
  - ~/.config/amp/checks/    — global (Amp-specific)

Frontmatter fields:
  - name: string (required)
  - description: string (optional)
  - severity-default: low | medium | high | critical (optional)
  - tools: [array of tool names] (optional, per-check tool restrictions)

Example:
  ---
  name: performance
  description: Flags common performance anti-patterns
  severity-default: medium
  tools: [Grep, Read]
  ---

  Look for these patterns:
  - Nested loops over the same collection

Execution model: Amp spawns a separate subagent per check during code review (parallel). Closer/project-level checks override same-named global checks.

## Provider Coverage (Research: 2026-04-08)

NATIVE CHECKS SUPPORT (structured format):
  - Amp: Full support. .agents/checks/ + ~/.config/agents/checks/. Amp-exclusive.

PROSE ANALOGUES (potential lossless conversion targets):
  - Claude Code: REVIEW.md (prose, no severity, no per-rule scoping)
    Source: Claude Code managed Code Review service docs
  - GitHub Copilot: .github/instructions/*.instructions.md (prose, path-scoped via glob)
    Source: https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/customizing-copilot/adding-custom-instructions-for-github-copilot
  - Cursor: .cursor/rules/*.mdc (prose, path-scoped)

NO EQUIVALENT:
  - Windsurf, Roo Code, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Codex, Zed, Cline

## Cross-Provider Path Status

.agents/checks/ is NOT a cross-provider shared convention (unlike .agents/skills/).
Only Amp uses this path. No other provider reads from it.

## Decision Required Before Implementation

Choose scope:
  Option 1 — Amp-only: model Amp's paths and format, no conversion. Clean and correct.
  Option 2 — Amp + prose analogues: Amp native + read support for REVIEW.md (Claude Code) and .github/instructions/ (Copilot). Convert to Amp checks format on export.
  Option 3 — Full canonical format: Define syllago's own canonical checks format, treat Amp as native, write bidirectional converters for prose analogues. Most aligned with hub-and-spoke architecture but conversion from prose to structured checks is hard without AI assistance.

## Related Work

- docs/research/agents-directory-cross-provider-research.md (full research doc)
- Source: agentskills.io spec (https://agentskills.io/specification)
- Amp manual: https://ampcode.com/manual
- OpenCode skills source: github.com/sst/opencode packages/opencode/src/skill/index.ts

title: Multi-provider loadout format

Design should cover: provider-specific overrides, partial install (skip unsupported types), rollback on failure, and the 'providers: all' magic string as equivalent to --to-all.

description: Run full classification and show what would be imported without writing anything. Supports admin validation workflows and policy review before any content lands on developer machines. Panel review N3.

title: Library table: show total item count
Automated provider compatibility monitoring
description:
Detect when AI coding tool providers change their configuration formats, hook events, MCP schemas, or content type support. Currently syllago relies on manual discovery. Automate this.

DESIGN: Two-tier monitoring — changelogs/releases for early signal, docs for format details.

## Tier 1: Changelog/Release Monitoring (Early Signal)

Changelogs surface changes before docs are updated. Monitor these for keywords like "hooks", "mcp", "rules", "config", "settings", "breaking".

| Provider | Changelog Source | Type | Fetchable? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Claude Code | https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog | Hosted docs | Yes (scrape) |
| Cursor | https://www.cursor.com/changelog | Hosted | Yes (scrape) |
| Gemini CLI | https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli/releases | GitHub releases | Yes (API) |
| Copilot CLI | https://github.com/github/docs (PRs to copilot/) | GitHub repo | Yes (API) |
| Windsurf | https://docs.windsurf.com (check for changelog) | Hosted docs | Yes (llms.txt) |
| Cline | https://github.com/cline/cline/releases | GitHub releases | Yes (API) |
| Roo Code | https://github.com/RooVetGit/Roo-Code/releases | GitHub releases | Yes (API) |
| OpenCode | https://github.com/opencode-ai/opencode/releases | GitHub releases | Yes (API) |
| Codex | https://developers.openai.com/codex (check for changelog) | Hosted docs | Yes (scrape) |
| Kiro | https://kiro.dev/docs/ (check for changelog/what's new) | Hosted docs | Unstable |
| Amp | https://ampcode.com/news | Blog/news | Yes (scrape) |
| Zed | https://zed.dev/releases | Hosted releases | Yes (scrape) |

## Tier 2: Doc Page Monitoring (Format Details)

After changelog flags a change, diff the actual format docs to understand what changed.

BEST RAW SOURCES (easiest to diff programmatically):
- Gemini CLI: raw.githubusercontent.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli/main/docs/*.md (BEST — raw markdown)
- Copilot CLI: github.com/github/docs/tree/main/content/copilot/ (raw markdown in public repo)
- Windsurf: docs.windsurf.com/llms.txt → *.md raw URLs (BEST — llms.txt index + raw markdown)
- OpenCode: opencode.ai/config.json (JSON Schema — machine-readable config format)
- Codex: developers.openai.com/codex/* (stable versioned docs)
- Zed: zed.dev/docs/ai/*.html (stable versioned docs)

HARDER TO DIFF (SPA or no raw source):
- Claude Code: code.claude.com/docs/en/* (no raw markdown, llms.txt available as index)
- Cursor: cursor.com/docs/* (SPA, no raw source)
- Cline: docs.cline.bot/* (no raw API found)
- Roo Code: docs.roocode.com/* (Docusaurus, check for docs repo)
- Kiro: kiro.dev/docs/* (no versioning, unstable)
- Amp: ampcode.com/manual/* (some pages need auth)

## Per-Provider Doc URLs (Format References)

### Claude Code (code.claude.com)
- Rules: /docs/en/memory, /docs/en/claude-directory
- Hooks: /docs/en/hooks (26 events incl WorktreeCreate, TeammateIdle, Elicitation)
- MCP: /docs/en/mcp
- Skills: /docs/en/skills
- Agents: /docs/en/sub-agents
- Commands: /docs/en/commands
- Settings: /docs/en/settings

### Cursor (cursor.com)
- Rules: /docs/rules (.mdc with frontmatter)
- Hooks: /docs/hooks (20 events — most of any provider)
- MCP: /docs/mcp (uses ${env:VAR} format)

### Gemini CLI (GitHub + geminicli.com)
- Config: github docs/reference/configuration.md
- Hooks: github docs/hooks/index.md (11 events)
- MCP: github docs/tools/mcp-server.md

### Copilot CLI (docs.github.com)
- Instructions: /en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/customize-copilot/add-custom-instructions
- Hooks: /en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/customize-copilot/use-hooks (6 events)
- MCP: /en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/customize-copilot/add-mcp-servers

### Windsurf (docs.windsurf.com)
- Rules: /windsurf/cascade/memories
- Hooks: /windsurf/cascade/hooks (12 events)
- MCP: /windsurf/cascade/mcp
- Skills: /windsurf/cascade/skills

### Cline (docs.cline.bot)
- Rules: /customization/cline-rules
- MCP: /mcp/configuring-mcp-servers
- No hooks, no skills

### Roo Code (docs.roocode.com)
- Rules: /features/custom-instructions
- Modes: /features/custom-modes
- MCP: /features/mcp/using-mcp-in-roo
- No hooks, no skills

### OpenCode (opencode.ai)
- Config: /docs/config/ + config.json (JSON Schema)
- Hooks/Plugins: /docs/plugins/ (25+ events via JS plugins)
- MCP: /docs/mcp-servers/
- Agents: /docs/agents/

### Codex (developers.openai.com)
- AGENTS.md: /codex/guides/agents-md
- Config: /codex/config-reference (TOML)
- Hooks: /codex/hooks (5 events, experimental)
- MCP: /codex/mcp
- Skills: /codex/skills

### Kiro (kiro.dev)
- Steering: /docs/steering/
- Hooks: /docs/hooks/ (10 events)
- MCP: /docs/mcp/configuration/

### Amp (ampcode.com)
- Manual: /manual (main reference)
- MCP: /news/lazy-load-mcp-with-skills, /news/mcp-permissions
- No hooks (has "checks" for code review)

### Zed (zed.dev)
- Rules: /docs/ai/rules
- MCP: /docs/ai/mcp (context_servers)
- Config: /docs/ai/configuration
- No hooks, no skills

## Implementation Sketch

1. Tracking file: .provider-monitor/baselines.json — stores per-URL content hash + last-checked timestamp
2. Script: provider-monitor.sh or Go command
   a. For each provider, fetch changelog/release page
   b. Hash content, compare to baseline
   c. If changed: extract diff, keyword-scan for relevant terms
   d. Create GH issue tagged "provider-change" with diff summary
   e. Update baseline
3. Trigger: GitHub Action on release (workflow_dispatch) or weekly cron
4. Human reviews issue, updates syllago if needed

KEY INSIGHT: Changelogs are the early warning system. Docs are the reference for implementation. Monitor changelogs frequently (weekly?), diff docs only when changelog flags a change.

NOTE: Docs lag behind code. A changelog entry like "Added WorktreeCreate hook event" may appear weeks before the docs page is updated. The monitoring system should flag changelog mentions even if the docs haven't changed yet.

title: Path reference rewriting during conversion
description: When converting content between providers, rewrite internal path references to point to correct provider-relative locations. rulesync issue 900 confirms this is an unresolved gap -- references like @.rulesync/rules/example.md stay as-is in generated output, breaking links. syllago should detect path references in content and map them to target provider paths during conversion. Important for loadouts with interconnected files.

title: Lockfile with integrity hashes and frozen mode for CI
description:
Evolve syllago's installed.json into a proper lockfile with content integrity verification and a CI-safe frozen mode.

RULESYNC APPROACH: rulesync-sources-lock.json pins exact commit SHAs + SHA-256 integrity hashes per skill. Three modes: normal (resolve+fetch if changed), --update (ignore lockfile), --frozen (fail if lockfile stale).

SYLLAGO APPROACH — iterate, don't copy:
- syllago already tracks installed content in installed.json with hashes
- Add integrity verification on install (warn if content changed at same version)
- Add a --check or --verify mode for CI that fails if installed content doesn't match lockfile
- DON'T call it --frozen (that's rulesync's flag name). Consider --strict, --verify, or --audit
- The lockfile should track: content hash, source registry, install timestamp, provider target, syllago version that installed it

WHY THIS MATTERS FOR ENTERPRISE:
- CI pipelines need deterministic builds
- Security teams want to verify that installed AI tool configs haven't been tampered with
- Audit trails for compliance (who installed what, when, from where)

RULESYNC GAPS (from issues): No explicit lockfile issues, but #1251 shows path bugs in curated skills that a proper lockfile+verification step would catch.

NOTE: This is infrastructure that enterprises expect. A --verify flag in CI is a differentiator over rulesync's --frozen because it can also validate content integrity, not just lockfile freshness.

title: Competitive landscape research: ai-rulez, block/ai-rules, rulesync
description:
Research completed 2026-03-27. Captures the competitive landscape for AI coding tool content managers.

KEY COMPETITORS:
1. rulesync (dyoshikawa) — 936 stars, 608K npm/mo, v7.23.0, TypeScript
   - Hub-and-spoke (same arch as syllago), 27 providers, 8 content types
   - DOES convert hooks (27 canonical events, 7 providers) and MCP (16 providers)
   - Has permissions content type, simulated features for non-native providers
   - Missing: loadouts, registry ecosystem, TUI, binary distribution (needs Node 22)
   
2. ai-rulez (Goldziher) — 95 stars, 404 npm/wk, v3.11.2, Go
   - Fan-out generator (NOT hub-and-spoke), 13 providers, 6 content types
   - NO hook conversion. MCP is simple pass-through.
   - Has: built-in content library (23 domains), token compression, MCP server mode, profiles
   - Missing: bidirectional conversion, hooks, loadouts, registry

3. block/ai-rules (Block/Square) — 83 stars, v1.5.1, Rust
   - Symlink farm (simplest arch), 11 providers, 4 content types
   - NO hooks, MCP is file copy only
   - Has: monorepo nested traversal, status/sync checking, enterprise backing
   - Missing: real conversion, hooks, MCP handling, loadouts, registry

SYLLAGO'S DURABLE ADVANTAGES:
- Loadouts (curated installable bundles) — no competitor has this
- Registry ecosystem (community content sharing) — no competitor has this
- TUI (interactive browsing/management) — no competitor has this
- Go binary (zero runtime deps) — rulesync needs Node 22, ai-rulez is Go too
- Hook conversion — shared with rulesync but syllago's spec is more formal

SYLLAGO'S GAPS VS RULESYNC:
- Provider count: 12 vs 27
- Hook event coverage: ~10 vs 27 canonical events
- Hook adapter count: 5 vs 7 providers
- No permissions content type
- No simulated features for non-native providers
- No shared+per-provider hook override pattern
- No Cursor env var format handling for MCP

STATUS: Research complete. Actionable items broken into separate beads.

title: Cursor MCP env var format conversion (env:VAR)
description:
Cursor uses a non-standard environment variable reference format in MCP configs.

ISSUE: Standard format is \${VAR}, Cursor uses \${env:VAR}.

RULESYNC IMPLEMENTATION:
- convertEnvToCursorFormat(): regex \$\{(?!env:)([^}:]+)\} -> \${env:\$1}
- convertEnvFromCursorFormat(): regex \$\{env:([^}]+)\} -> \${\$1}
- Applied bidirectionally during MCP canonicalize and render

CHECK: Does syllago already handle this? If not, Cursor MCP configs with env var references will break.

title: Shared + per-provider override pattern for canonical hooks
description:
Consider adopting a pattern where the canonical hook config has shared hooks AND per-provider override sections.

RULESYNC VALIDATION: Issue #1259 shows their per-provider frontmatter overrides DON'T always work — Google Antigravity trigger declarations are silently ignored. Issue #1181 shows opt-in/opt-out granularity is insufficient — Copilot always gets agent/runSubagent injected with no way to disable it.

SYLLAGO APPROACH: Don't copy rulesync's approach directly. Their overrides are scattered across frontmatter in individual files, making it hard to debug when they fail silently. Instead, consider a centralized override mechanism:

Option A: Per-provider sections in the canonical hook config (rulesync's pattern but centralized)
  { "hooks": {...}, "overrides": { "claude-code": {...}, "cursor": {...} } }

Option B: Provider-scoped hooks in loadout manifests (syllago-native)
  Since loadouts already target specific providers, the loadout manifest could declare provider-specific hook variations. This keeps the canonical format clean and moves provider concerns to the distribution layer.

Option C: Defer this entirely
  The spec defines canonical hooks. If a provider can't handle a hook, the adapter drops it with a warning. No override mechanism needed — users create provider-specific content when needed.

RECOMMENDATION: Start with Option C (simplest). Add overrides only when real user demand emerges. rulesync's issues show that override mechanisms create their own bugs.

title: Shared hook converter config pattern
description:
Refactor hook adapters so most providers use a shared converter with a config struct rather than each adapter implementing full conversion logic independently.

RULESYNC VALIDATION: Their shared converter (tool-hooks-converter.ts) works well for Claude Code and Factory Droid, but Cursor, Gemini, OpenCode, and Copilot each needed custom implementations anyway. Issue #1250 shows the shared converter's command prefixing logic had bugs with bare executables (npx, prettier, etc.).

SYLLAGO APPROACH: Don't over-abstract. The shared converter pattern is valuable but the config struct should be SIMPLER than rulesync's. Focus on what's actually shared:

Shared behavior (put in common converter):
- Event name lookup (canonical <-> provider)
- Timeout unit conversion (spec seconds -> provider milliseconds)
- Basic command validation

Provider-specific behavior (keep in adapters):
- Output format (JSON settings merge, standalone file, JS plugin)
- Path prefixing logic (each provider has different project dir vars and rules)
- Matcher support/stripping (varies per provider and per event)

The lesson from rulesync #1250: command prefixing is harder than it looks. Don't try to share path resolution logic — let each adapter handle its own paths. Share only the stateless mapping tables.

title: Restrict allowed GitHub Actions to specific orgs
description:
## Why
allowed_actions is set to "all" — any GitHub Action from any author can be used in workflows. While SHA pinning is enforced (good), restricting to known-trusted orgs adds defense-in-depth against supply chain attacks through new workflow additions.

## What
Change allowed_actions from "all" to "selected" and specify trusted action orgs.

## How
- Settings > Actions > General > Actions permissions > Allow select actions
- Allow actions from: actions/*, github/*, sigstore/*, anthropics/*, ossf/*, anchore/*, golangci/*, mitchellh/*
- This still requires SHA pinning (already enforced) but limits which orgs' actions can be referenced

## Success Criteria
- Only actions from approved orgs can be used in workflows
- Existing workflows continue to work (all current actions are from these orgs)

## Blocked By
Repo must be public for this to matter (private repos have limited external exposure)

title: Enable secret scanning AI detection and validity checks
description:
## Why
secret_scanning_ai_detection and secret_scanning_validity_checks are both disabled. AI detection catches non-standard secret patterns (custom API keys, internal tokens). Validity checks verify detected secrets are actually active/valid, reducing false positives.

## What
Enable both features in repo security settings.

## How
- Settings > Security > Code security and analysis
- Enable "Use AI detection to find additional secrets" 
- Enable "Automatically verify if a secret is valid by sending it to the relevant partner"

## Success Criteria
- Both features show as enabled in repo settings
- Secret scanning catches a broader set of patterns

## Blocked By
Repo must be public (or have GitHub Advanced Security for private repos)

title: Enable required conversation resolution on main
description:
## Why
required_conversation_resolution is false. Review comments can be ignored without being resolved before merge. Once public with external contributors, this means security concerns raised in review could be merged without addressing them.

## What
Enable required conversation resolution on the main branch protection rule.

## How
- Settings > Branches > main > Require conversation resolution before merging

## Success Criteria
- All review conversation threads must be resolved before PR can merge

## Blocked By
Repo must be public for full effect with external contributors

title: Audit pull_request_target usage in pr-policy.yml
description:
## Why
pr-policy.yml uses pull_request_target which runs with repo write permissions against the base branch. Currently safe — only runs the vouch check action, doesn't checkout PR head code for execution. But once public, external contributors' PRs will trigger this workflow with write access to the repo.

## What
Verify the workflow remains safe for external PRs, or switch to pull_request trigger if possible.

## How
- Review: the current workflow only runs mitchellh/vouch/action/check-pr which reads PR metadata. It does NOT checkout or execute PR code. This is safe.
- The checkout step checks out the base branch (default for pull_request_target), not the PR head. Safe.
- Document this decision so future modifications don't accidentally introduce a vulnerability
- Consider adding a comment in the workflow file explaining why pull_request_target is used and the safety constraints

## Success Criteria
- Workflow is documented as safe for external contributors
- No code from the PR head is executed in the pull_request_target context

## Blocked By
Repo must be public for external contributor PRs to test this

title: Enable GitHub Code Security for CodeQL scanning
description:
## Why
CodeQL workflow runs on every push but fails with: "Code Security must be enabled for this repository to use code scanning." The analysis completes but results can't be uploaded. This will resolve once the repo is public, but needs explicit enablement.

## What
Enable Code Security features in GitHub repo settings so CodeQL can upload scan results.

## How (general)
- Go to repo Settings > Security > Code security and analysis
- Enable "Code scanning" (requires public repo or GitHub Advanced Security license)
- Alternatively: remove the CodeQL workflow until the repo is public, then re-enable

## Success Criteria
- CodeQL workflow completes without errors
- Code scanning results appear in the Security tab

## Blocked By
Repo must be public (or have GitHub Advanced Security enabled for private repos)

title: Fix OSSF Scorecard token permissions
description:
## Why
Scorecard workflow fails with: "Resource not accessible by integration" when trying to list commits via GraphQL. The GITHUB_TOKEN doesn't have sufficient scope for the OSSF Scorecard action's GraphQL queries. This will work differently once the repo is public.

## What
Configure proper token permissions for the OSSF Scorecard GitHub Action.

## How (general)
- Option A (public repo): Once public, the default GITHUB_TOKEN with contents:read should work
- Option B (if still failing): Create a PAT with public_repo scope, add as SCORECARD_TOKEN secret
- Option C: Add explicit permissions to the workflow: contents:read, id-token:write
- Scorecard docs: https://github.com/ossf/scorecard-action#authentication

## Success Criteria
- Scorecard workflow completes without errors
- Scorecard badge/results are available

## Blocked By
Repo must be public for default token to work with Scorecard's GraphQL queries

title: M9: Proxy allowedPorts stored but never enforced
description:
## Why
The Proxy struct stores allowedPorts from --allow-port flags, but handleConn() only checks allowedDomains via isAllowed(). The port allowlist is never consulted. Users configuring --allow-port get a false sense of security — if localhost is in the domain allowlist, ALL ports are accessible.

## What
Implement port-based filtering in the sandbox proxy.

## How (general)
- In proxy.go handleConn(), after the host allowlist check, extract the port from req.Host
- If the host resolves to 127.0.0.1/localhost, verify the port is in allowedPorts
- If allowedPorts is empty and the host is allowed, permit all ports (backwards compatible)
- If allowedPorts is non-empty, only permit listed ports

## Success Criteria
- --allow-port flag actually restricts which ports are accessible
- Connections to non-allowed ports on localhost are blocked
- Connections to allowed ports succeed
- Backwards compatible when no --allow-port flags are specified

## Testing
- Unit test: proxy with allowedPorts=[3000], connect to localhost:3000 — allowed
- Unit test: proxy with allowedPorts=[3000], connect to localhost:8080 — blocked
- Unit test: proxy with empty allowedPorts, connect to any allowed host — all ports allowed
- Integration test: sandbox with --allow-port 3000 blocks port 8080

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — Section M9
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

title: M8: Sandbox auto-approves low-risk config changes via denylist
description:
## Why
After sandbox exit, config changes are classified as high-risk (mcpServers, hooks, commands) or low-risk. Low-risk changes are auto-applied without prompting. The denylist only checks three hardcoded strings. A provider could add new executable content types that syllago doesn't know about, and they'd be auto-approved as low-risk.

## What
Invert the sandbox config diff logic from denylist to allowlist.

## How (general)
- In sandbox/runner.go and configdiff.go, change isHighRiskDiff to use an allowlist approach
- Define explicitly-known-safe keys that can be auto-approved (e.g., theme, editor settings)
- Everything not on the safe list requires user prompt
- Alternatively: display all changes and auto-approve after brief delay with cancel option

## Success Criteria
- Unknown/new config keys require user approval after sandbox exit
- Known-safe keys (non-executable settings) are auto-approved
- Users see what changes are being applied even for auto-approved changes

## Testing
- Unit test: config change to known-safe key — auto-approved
- Unit test: config change to unknown key — user prompted
- Unit test: config change to mcpServers/hooks/commands — user prompted (regression)
- Unit test: new arbitrary key not in allowlist — user prompted

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — Section M8
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

title: Extract testable logic from registryCreateFromNative (0%)
description:
**File:** registry_create_native.go
**Function:** registryCreateFromNative (line 19, 0%)
**Also covers:** promptUserScopedHooks (line 272, 0%)
**Blocker:** interactive-prompt (multi-step stdin via bufio throughout lines 55-118)
**Pattern:** var-seam + extract-pure-logic
**Effort:** L (> 90 min)

Multi-step interactive flow:
- Scans native provider content
- Prompts user to select provider or individual items
- Prompts about user-scoped hooks
- Creates registry scaffold with selected items

Recommended approach:
1. selectByProvider and selectIndividualItems already accept *bufio.Scanner — covered by separate bead
2. Extract the orchestration logic: given scan results + user selections → registry scaffold
3. promptUserScopedHooks: reads home dir settings + prompts via scanner. Needs home dir fixtures + scanner injection.
4. Test the orchestration with pre-determined selections (no interactive prompts)

Note: os.Getwd() dependency — tests need chdir with t.Cleanup restore.

title: Add interface seam for reprobeRegistryVisibility (0%)
description:
**File:** registry_cmd.go
**Function:** reprobeRegistryVisibility (line 607, 0%)
**Blocker:** missing-seam (calls registry.ProbeVisibility which hits network)
**Pattern:** interface-inject or var-seam
**Effort:** M (30-90 min)

Calls registry.ProbeVisibility() to check if a registry URL is public/private.
This does HTTP requests — can't unit test without a seam.

Options:
1. **var-seam:** var probeVisibility = registry.ProbeVisibility — override in tests
2. **interface-inject:** Accept a Prober interface — more Go-idiomatic but heavier refactor
3. **httptest:** If ProbeVisibility accepts an HTTP client or URL, use httptest.NewServer

Recommended: var-seam (simplest, matches existing patterns like findProjectRoot).

Test cases:
- Public registry (probe returns public)
- Private registry (probe returns private)
- Probe error (network failure)
- Config update after visibility change

Also need to test the config-update logic that runs after the probe.

title: Extract testable logic from runLoadoutCreate (0%)
description:
**File:** loadout_create.go
**Function:** runLoadoutCreate (line 27, 0%)
**Blocker:** interactive-prompt (multi-step stdin via bufio.NewScanner throughout lines 48-178)
**Pattern:** var-seam + extract-pure-logic
**Effort:** L (> 90 min)

Deeply interactive multi-step wizard reading from os.Stdin:
- Prompts for loadout name
- Prompts for target provider
- Prompts for content selection
- Prompts for description

Recommended approach:
1. Extract pure logic into a separate function: buildLoadoutFromInputs(name, provider, items, desc) that takes all user inputs as parameters and returns the manifest. Test that function thoroughly.
2. For the interactive flow itself, accept io.Reader for stdin injection:
   var stdinReader io.Reader = os.Stdin  // var-seam
   scanner := bufio.NewScanner(stdinReader)
3. Test interactive flow with strings.NewReader simulating multi-line input.

Note: The loadout package already has BuildManifest() + WriteManifest() — check if runLoadoutCreate duplicates logic that could be consolidated.

title: Pre-allocate slice capacity in catalog filter methods
description:
Five catalog filtering methods append to empty slices without pre-allocating capacity, causing multiple re-allocations per filter call:

- ByType() (types.go:112)
- ByTypeShared() (types.go:132)
- ByRegistry() (types.go:143)
- ByTypeLibrary() (types.go:165)
- libraryItems() in app.go (line 528)

Fix: Use make([]ContentItem, 0, len(c.Items)/4) or similar heuristic capacity. For methods called in hot paths (TUI sidebar refresh), this reduces GC pressure.

Files: cli/internal/catalog/types.go, cli/internal/tui/app.go
close_reason: Closed

title: Use pointer receivers for ContentItem methods
description:
ContentItem is an 11-field struct (5 strings, 1 slice, 1 pointer, 1 bool, 1 ContentType). Both methods (IsExample, IsBuiltin) use value receivers, copying all 11 fields on every call.

Fix: Change to pointer receivers:
- func (ci *ContentItem) IsExample() bool
- func (ci *ContentItem) IsBuiltin() bool

Also audit ByType, ByTypeShared, ByRegistry, ByTypeLibrary — these return []ContentItem (value slices). Consider returning []*ContentItem for consistency if callers don't mutate.

Files: cli/internal/catalog/types.go
close_reason: Closed

title: Evaluate and remove legacy single-file scanner format
description:
The catalog scanner (scanner.go:425-502) supports two formats:
1. Directory-per-item (new): <type>/<provider>/<item-name>/ with content + .syllago.yaml
2. Single file (legacy): <type>/<provider>/<file> with .syllago.<file>.yaml alongside

If the transition to directory-per-item is complete and no existing content relies on the legacy format, remove the legacy code path to reduce maintenance surface.

Investigation needed:
- Check if any existing registries or library content uses single-file format
- Check if add/import ever creates single-file format items
